title: 好奇心科学
subtitle: 给十岁孩子的生活科学小故事
description: 给十岁孩子的生活科学故事库：从身体、厨房、家、天空、大地、动物、植物、化学、机器到城市材料，每条都有中英文对照和朗读。

categories.title: 身体的秘密
categories.subtitle: 从头到脚都是有趣的小机器

stories.title_zh: 眼睛是怎么看见东西的？
stories.title_en: How do our eyes see things?
stories.answer_zh: 光从外面射进眼睛，先穿过透明的角膜和晶状体，被聚焦到眼球后面的一层叫视网膜的薄膜上。视网膜上有非常多的小细胞，它们把光变成电信号，沿着视神经送到大脑，大脑再把信号拼起来，告诉你看到的是猫还是苹果。
stories.answer_en: Light comes from outside, passes through your clear cornea and lens, and lands on a thin layer at the back of your eye called the retina. Tiny cells there turn the light into electric signals, send them through the optic nerve to your brain, and your brain pieces them together so you know it's a cat or an apple.
stories.fun: 闭上一只眼睛，再用一根手指伸到面前——你会发现判断距离变难了，因为我们靠两只眼睛的小差别来判断远近。

stories.title_zh: 为什么我们有两只眼睛？
stories.title_en: Why do we have two eyes?
stories.answer_zh: 两只眼睛之间有一点距离，所以它们看到的画面会有一点点不一样。大脑把两幅画面合并起来，就能判断东西离我们有多远，这叫立体视觉。一只眼睛也能看，但判断远近就没那么准了。
stories.answer_en: Your two eyes sit a little apart, so each one sees a slightly different picture. Your brain combines the two pictures and that's how you judge how far away things are. It's called stereo vision. With only one eye you can still see, but judging distance is much harder.
stories.fun: 球类运动员的两只眼睛配合特别好，所以他们能在球飞过来时迅速判断要跑几步。

stories.title_zh: 耳朵里面是什么样子的？
stories.title_en: What's inside your ear?
stories.answer_zh: 耳朵外面的那块叫耳廓，像一个小漏斗，把声音收进耳道。声音让耳膜像鼓面一样振动，再带动里面三块小骨头，最后传到一个像蜗牛的器官，叫耳蜗，里面有许多小细胞，把振动变成信号送到大脑。
stories.answer_en: The outer part of your ear works like a funnel that catches sound. The sound makes your eardrum vibrate, which moves three tiny bones, and finally reaches a snail-shaped part called the cochlea. Hair cells inside the cochlea turn the vibrations into signals that travel to your brain.
stories.fun: 人体里最小的骨头就藏在耳朵里，最小的那块叫镫骨，比米粒还小。

stories.title_zh: 我们是怎么闻到味道的？
stories.title_en: How do we smell things?
stories.answer_zh: 鲜花、面包、水果会向空气里释放非常小的气味分子。这些分子飘进鼻子，落在鼻腔顶部的一小片区域上，那里有专门感受气味的细胞，把信息送到大脑。大脑会把这些信号和你记忆中的味道对上，告诉你这是花香还是烤面包的味道。
stories.answer_en: Flowers, bread and fruit release very tiny smell particles into the air. They drift into your nose and land on a small patch high inside your nostrils where smell cells live. Those cells send signals to your brain, which matches them with smells you've met before — that's how you recognise flowers or fresh bread.
stories.fun: 感冒鼻子塞住时，吃饭觉得没味道，是因为气味分子飘不上去，舌头其实只能尝出五种基本味道。

stories.title_zh: 舌头能尝到几种味道？
stories.title_en: How many tastes can your tongue feel?
stories.answer_zh: 科学家发现，舌头上的味蕾主要能分辨五种味道：甜、咸、酸、苦，还有一种叫鲜味，比如鸡汤和西红柿的那种深沉味道。其他复杂的味道，比如薄荷的清凉、辣椒的辣，其实是触觉和温度感觉，不是真正的味道。
stories.answer_en: Scientists have found that taste buds on your tongue can pick out five basic tastes: sweet, salty, sour, bitter, and a savoury one called umami, like the rich taste of chicken soup or tomatoes. Mint's cool feel and chilli's burn are actually touch and temperature, not real tastes.
stories.fun: 辣并不是味道，而是辣椒里的辣椒素让舌头上的温度神经误以为很烫。

stories.title_zh: 皮肤为什么能感觉到冷和热？
stories.title_en: Why can our skin feel cold and hot?
stories.answer_zh: 皮肤里藏着很多种小小的感受器，有的专门感觉触碰，有的感觉冷，有的感觉热，有的感觉痛。你摸一杯热水，皮肤里的热感受器就向大脑发信号说"烫"。这套系统能保护我们，提醒我们赶快把手挪开。
stories.answer_en: Your skin holds many tiny sensors. Some feel touch, some feel cold, some feel heat, and some feel pain. When you touch a hot cup, the heat sensors quickly send a 'hot!' signal to your brain. That warning system protects you by telling you to pull your hand back fast.
stories.fun: 嘴唇和指尖的感受器最密集，所以小宝宝喜欢用嘴巴探索世界。

stories.title_zh: 为什么我们晕车？
stories.title_en: Why do we get carsick?
stories.answer_zh: 你的眼睛告诉大脑"我没动"，但耳朵里负责平衡的小器官却感觉到车在摇晃。大脑收到不一致的信号就糊涂了，于是用恶心和头晕来抗议。看远处的山或路面通常能缓解，因为眼睛和耳朵的信号又对上了。
stories.answer_en: Your eyes tell your brain 'I'm not moving', but the balance organs in your ears feel the car rocking. The brain gets two messages that don't match and complains by making you feel dizzy or sick. Looking far ahead at the road or hills usually helps, because then your eyes and ears agree again.
stories.fun: 宇航员一开始进太空也会晕，因为身体的平衡感完全乱了。

stories.title_zh: 为什么有的人对花粉过敏？
stories.title_en: Why are some people allergic to pollen?
stories.answer_zh: 我们身体有一支保护我们不被坏东西伤害的军队，叫免疫系统。它会去对付细菌和病毒。可是有些人的免疫系统太敏感了，把花粉这种没害的东西也当成敌人来打仗，结果让人打喷嚏、流眼泪、鼻子痒。
stories.answer_en: Your body has a defence team called the immune system that fights germs. In some people the team is a bit too alert and treats harmless things like pollen as enemies. The fight then shows up as sneezing, itchy noses and watery eyes.
stories.fun: 蜜蜂帮花传粉时，身上沾满了花粉，所以它们其实在帮过敏的人传播"敌人"。

stories.title_zh: 为什么吃冰淇淋会让头疼？
stories.title_en: Why does ice cream sometimes give us a headache?
stories.answer_zh: 上颚被冰冷的东西碰到时，那里的血管会突然收缩、然后又张开。大脑误以为是头部出了问题，就用一阵短短的头痛来提醒你。这种痛通常只持续几秒到十几秒就过去了。
stories.answer_en: When the roof of your mouth is hit by something very cold, the blood vessels there suddenly tighten and then open up again. Your brain misreads the signal as a problem near your head and gives you a short headache as a warning. It usually fades in just a few seconds.
stories.fun: 下次再被"冰激凌头痛"袭击时，把舌头贴在上颚帮它暖一暖，往往会好得快一些。

stories.title_zh: 为什么洋葱让人哭？
stories.title_en: Why does cutting onions make us cry?
stories.answer_zh: 切洋葱时，洋葱里面的两种物质混合起来，会变成一种带刺激性的气体跑到空气里。它碰到眼睛里的水，就形成一点点像稀稀的硫酸的东西。眼睛感觉刺痛，赶紧分泌眼泪把它冲走。
stories.answer_en: When you cut an onion, two chemicals inside it mix and float into the air as a stinging gas. The gas meets the water on your eyes and turns into something a bit like very weak acid. Your eyes feel the sting and quickly make tears to wash it away.
stories.fun: 把洋葱先在冰箱里冷藏一会，气体跑出来就慢得多，切起来眼泪就少了。

stories.title_zh: 为什么我们看见食物会流口水？
stories.title_en: Why do we drool when we see food?
stories.answer_zh: 我们的身体非常聪明：看到食物时，大脑就提前发命令，让嘴里的口水腺多分泌一些口水。口水里有湿润食物的水分，也有一点点消化淀粉的酶，让我们一咬下去就能开始消化。
stories.answer_en: Your body is clever: just seeing food tells your brain to get the saliva glands ready. They make more spit to moisten the food and a little enzyme that begins breaking down starches the moment you take a bite.
stories.fun: 巴甫洛夫的小狗听到喂食的铃声就会流口水，这种学习叫"条件反射"。

stories.title_zh: 为什么挠胳肢窝那么痒？
stories.title_en: Why is it so ticklish under our arms?
stories.answer_zh: 胳肢窝、脚底这些地方平时被身体保护起来，皮肤更薄，神经也比较密集。别人挠你的时候，大脑因为不知道下一秒会发生什么，就用笑和"痒"来反应。自己挠自己反而不痒，因为大脑早就预料到了。
stories.answer_en: Soft places like your armpits and the soles of your feet are usually tucked away. Their skin is thinner and packed with nerves. When someone else tickles you, your brain can't predict what's next, so it reacts with laughter. When you tickle yourself, your brain already knows it's coming, so it doesn't tickle as much.
stories.fun: 猩猩被挠胳肢窝也会发出类似笑的声音，所以这是哺乳动物共有的反应。

stories.title_zh: 为什么刚出生的宝宝只能喝奶？
stories.title_en: Why can newborn babies only drink milk?
stories.answer_zh: 宝宝刚出生时，牙齿还没长出来，胃肠道也很娇嫩，没办法消化米饭、肉这些固体食物。母乳和配方奶很温和，里面有蛋白质、脂肪和糖，正好够宝宝长身体。等几个月后，胃肠强壮一些了，才能开始加米糊和蔬菜泥。
stories.answer_en: Newborn babies don't have teeth yet and their stomachs are very delicate, so they can't break down rice or meat. Breast milk and formula are gentle and packed with the right protein, fat and sugar for growing. After a few months, when their guts are stronger, they can start trying rice cereal and mashed vegetables.
stories.fun: 母乳的味道会随着妈妈吃的东西略有变化，宝宝其实在妈妈肚子里就在"提前试菜"。

stories.title_zh: 食物在身体里要走多远？
stories.title_en: How far does food travel inside us?
stories.answer_zh: 你咽下去的一口饭，先经过大约二十五厘米长的食管到达胃。然后进入小肠，弯弯曲曲大约六米长，养分在这里被吸收。最后进入大肠，里面还有大约一米半。整段旅程加起来比你身高的好几倍还长。
stories.answer_en: A single bite of rice first slides about twenty-five centimetres down your food pipe to the stomach. Then it enters the small intestine, which is six metres of coils that suck up nutrients. Finally it reaches the large intestine, another metre and a half. The whole trip is many times longer than you are tall.
stories.fun: 把成人的小肠拉直，差不多和一辆校车一样长。

stories.title_zh: 为什么饿了肚子会咕咕叫？
stories.title_en: Why does your tummy growl when you're hungry?
stories.answer_zh: 胃和肠子总是会做小小的肌肉运动，把里面的食物往前推。当胃里空了，里面只剩下空气和液体，这些运动就把空气挤来挤去，发出咕咕的声音。其实饱饱的时候肚子也在动，只不过有食物在，声音被挡住了。
stories.answer_en: Your stomach and intestines are always doing little muscle squeezes to push food along. When the stomach is empty there's only air and juice inside, and the squeezing makes the air gurgle around. Your tummy keeps moving when it's full too — the food just muffles the sound.
stories.fun: 这种规律的肠胃蠕动有个好听的名字，叫"蠕动波"。

stories.title_zh: 为什么会打嗝？
stories.title_en: Why do we hiccup?
stories.answer_zh: 胸腔和肚子中间有一片大肌肉叫横膈膜，它帮助我们呼吸。吃得太急、喝汽水、突然大笑都可能让它突然收缩一下，让气体快速吸进喉咙，再被声带挡住，就发出"嗝"的一声。
stories.answer_en: Between your chest and your belly sits a big muscle called the diaphragm that helps you breathe. Eating too fast, drinking fizzy drinks or laughing hard can make it suddenly jerk. Air rushes into your throat and gets stopped by your vocal cords, making the 'hic' sound.
stories.fun: 宝宝在妈妈肚子里就会打嗝，妈妈能从外面感觉到一阵一阵的小跳动。

stories.title_zh: 为什么我们必须喝水？
stories.title_en: Why do we have to drink water?
stories.answer_zh: 我们身体大约百分之六十是水。血液靠水流动，关节靠水润滑，脑子里也有水。每天通过汗水、呼吸和上厕所，我们会失去大约两升水，所以必须喝水补回来，不然就会感到口干、头晕、没力气。
stories.answer_en: About sixty percent of your body is water. Your blood flows with water, your joints slide on water and even your brain is mostly water. Every day you lose about two litres through sweat, breathing and the toilet, so you need to drink to top it up — otherwise you feel dry, dizzy and tired.
stories.fun: 西瓜里九成都是水，所以夏天吃西瓜也能帮你补水。

stories.title_zh: 为什么我们要拉便便？
stories.title_en: Why do we have to poop?
stories.answer_zh: 食物在肠子里走完一圈后，营养已经被身体吸收掉了，剩下的是吸收不了的纤维、死掉的细菌、脱落的细胞等。身体把这些攒到一起送出去，才不会在肚子里腐烂。所以便便其实是身体的"清理工作"。
stories.answer_en: After food finishes its trip through your intestines, the nutrients have been absorbed. What's left is fibre your body can't use, dead bacteria and worn-out cells. Your body packs them together and sends them out so they don't rot inside. Pooping is really just the body's cleaning shift.
stories.fun: 健康的便便差不多七成是水，剩下的才是固体。

stories.title_zh: 尿是从哪里来的？
stories.title_en: Where does pee come from?
stories.answer_zh: 我们后腰两侧各有一个像蚕豆形状的肾。肾每天像净水器一样过滤掉血液里的废物和多余的水，攒成尿，再通过两根细管送到膀胱里储存起来。膀胱满了，大脑就告诉你：该上厕所啦。
stories.answer_en: On each side of your lower back sits a bean-shaped organ called a kidney. Your kidneys filter your blood like a water purifier, taking out waste and extra water and turning them into urine. Two thin tubes carry the urine to your bladder, and when the bladder is full your brain says: time for the toilet.
stories.fun: 如果不喝水，肾就要把尿浓缩得很黄；多喝水尿就变浅，颜色其实是健康的小信号。

stories.title_zh: 为什么晚上需要睡觉？
stories.title_en: Why do we need to sleep at night?
stories.answer_zh: 白天大脑努力工作，存了一天的信息，身体也用了很多能量。睡觉时，大脑会整理记忆，把重要的东西保存下来；身体会修复肌肉、长高个子、补充能量。所以睡得好的小朋友学得快、长得快、心情也好。
stories.answer_en: During the day your brain works hard and stores tons of information, and your body burns lots of energy. While you sleep, your brain sorts memories and saves the important ones, and your body repairs muscles, grows taller and refills its energy. That's why kids who sleep well learn faster and feel happier.
stories.fun: 十岁左右的小朋友每晚最好睡九到十一个小时。

stories.title_zh: 我们为什么会做梦？
stories.title_en: Why do we dream?
stories.answer_zh: 睡觉时大脑分成几个阶段，其中有一段叫"快速眼动睡眠"，眼睛在闭着的眼皮下快速转动，大脑非常活跃。科学家认为，做梦可能是大脑在整理白天的经历、练习处理情绪、检查记忆。所以做梦是大脑的小练习。
stories.answer_en: Sleep moves through several stages, and one of them is called REM, when your eyes flick about under closed lids and your brain becomes very active. Scientists think dreams help the brain sort through the day, practise handling feelings and tidy up memories. Dreaming is the brain's little workout.
stories.fun: 差不多每九十分钟就有一次做梦的高峰，所以一晚上其实做了好几次梦。

stories.title_zh: 为什么会打哈欠？
stories.title_en: Why do we yawn?
stories.answer_zh: 我们困了、无聊、刚起床时常常会打哈欠。哈欠时嘴巴张得大大的，吸进大量空气，可能有助于让大脑稍微凉快一些，也能让我们更清醒一点。最神奇的是，看见别人打哈欠，自己也忍不住跟着打，可能是因为我们对别人的表情很敏感。
stories.answer_en: We often yawn when we're sleepy, bored or just waking up. A big yawn pulls in a lot of air, which may cool the brain a little and help us feel more awake. The funny thing is, watching someone else yawn often makes you yawn too — maybe because humans are very tuned in to each other's faces.
stories.fun: 连狗、猫，甚至鱼都会打哈欠，并不只是人类的习惯。

stories.title_zh: 饿了为什么会没力气？
stories.title_en: Why do we feel weak when hungry?
stories.answer_zh: 身体的肌肉和大脑都靠血糖来工作。吃完饭血糖升高，过几个小时血糖慢慢降下来，大脑就觉得需要补充能量了。如果你忍着不吃，肌肉和脑子缺燃料，就会觉得手抖、头晕、注意力差。
stories.answer_en: Your muscles and brain run on sugar carried in your blood. After a meal your blood sugar is high; a few hours later it drops, and your brain knows it needs more fuel. If you skip eating, your muscles and brain run low and you start to feel shaky, dizzy and unable to focus.
stories.fun: 大脑虽然小，但它一天消耗的能量大约占整个身体的五分之一。

stories.title_zh: 为什么吃太多会想睡觉？
stories.title_en: Why does a big meal make us sleepy?
stories.answer_zh: 吃饱后，身体要把很多血液送到胃和肠子帮忙消化，其他地方分到的血就少了一些。吃含碳水化合物多的食物时，身体还会让一种叫"血清素"的小信号增多，让人感到放松、想休息。
stories.answer_en: After a big meal your body sends extra blood to your stomach and intestines to help with digestion, so other parts get a little less for a while. A meal with lots of carbs also bumps up a brain messenger called serotonin that makes you feel calm and ready to rest.
stories.fun: 这就是为什么吃完午饭再上数学课，总觉得眼皮特别重。

stories.title_zh: 为什么我们一年要长高几厘米？
stories.title_en: Why do we grow several centimetres taller every year?
stories.answer_zh: 小朋友的骨头两端有一段软软的部分，叫生长板。大脑里的脑垂体会分泌一种叫生长激素的信号，让生长板上的细胞不停制造新的骨头，骨头就慢慢变长了。等到十几岁以后，生长板会渐渐变硬，人就停止长高了。
stories.answer_en: Children's bones have soft zones near the ends called growth plates. The pituitary gland in your brain sends out growth hormone, which tells the cells in those zones to keep making new bone. After your teen years the plates harden and growing tall stops.
stories.fun: 生长激素大部分是在你深度睡觉时分泌的，所以早睡真的能帮你长高。

stories.title_zh: 心脏为什么一直跳？
stories.title_en: Why does the heart never stop beating?
stories.answer_zh: 心脏是一块特别厉害的肌肉，它有自己的小指挥官——一群叫窦房结的细胞，会按节奏发出电信号，让心脏一收一缩。每跳一下，就把血液送到身体各个角落。它不需要我们提醒，从我们出生前就开始工作，一直工作一辈子。
stories.answer_en: Your heart is a very special muscle with its own little conductor — a group of cells called the sinoatrial node — that sends out steady electric signals telling the heart to squeeze. Every beat pushes blood to every corner of your body. It started working before you were born and doesn't need you to remind it.
stories.fun: 一个十岁孩子的心脏一天大约要跳十万次，一年差不多三千八百万次。

stories.title_zh: 血液为什么是红色的？
stories.title_en: Why is blood red?
stories.answer_zh: 血液里有很多红细胞，每个红细胞里都装着一种叫血红蛋白的分子，里面有铁。铁碰到氧气后会变成亮亮的红色，这就是动脉血看起来红润的原因。当血液把氧气送出去后，颜色会变成暗红，但还是红色。
stories.answer_en: Your blood is full of red cells, and each one carries a molecule called haemoglobin that contains iron. When iron meets oxygen it turns a bright red, which is why blood from arteries looks so vivid. After the blood drops off the oxygen it turns darker red, but still red.
stories.fun: 螃蟹、章鱼的血却是淡蓝色的，因为它们用铜来运氧。

stories.title_zh: 为什么我们要呼吸？
stories.title_en: Why do we have to breathe?
stories.answer_zh: 我们的身体每时每刻都在用氧气把吃下去的食物"烧"成能量。氧气从肺进入血液，血液把氧气送到每一个细胞。同时，细胞会产生废气二氧化碳，跟着血液回到肺，再被呼出去。所以呼吸是给身体加油，也是排废气。
stories.answer_en: Your cells use oxygen non-stop to 'burn' food into energy. Oxygen enters the blood through your lungs and travels everywhere. The leftover gas, carbon dioxide, hitchhikes back in the blood and leaves through your lungs. Breathing in is fuel; breathing out is the rubbish truck.
stories.fun: 成年人一天大约要吸入两万次空气，大约一万一千升。

stories.title_zh: 运动时为什么心跳变快？
stories.title_en: Why does your heart pound faster when you run?
stories.answer_zh: 跑步、踢球时，肌肉用力工作，需要更多的氧气和能量。大脑给心脏发命令：跳快一点，把血液和氧气更多更快地送出去。所以心跳变快是身体在告诉自己"现在很努力"。
stories.answer_en: When you run or kick a ball, your muscles need extra oxygen and fuel. Your brain orders your heart to beat faster so it can deliver more blood — and oxygen — quickly. A pounding heart is your body's way of saying 'we're working hard now'.
stories.fun: 经常运动的人，心脏更强，平时不动时心跳反而会比较慢，这是健康的表现。

stories.title_zh: 为什么憋气憋不了太久？
stories.title_en: Why can't we hold our breath for very long?
stories.answer_zh: 身体里二氧化碳越积越多时，大脑里有一个监测器就会很紧张，逼你赶快换气。所以憋气难受的不是缺氧，而是二氧化碳太多。专业潜水员经过训练可以暂时忽视这个信号，但普通人很快就受不了。
stories.answer_en: When carbon dioxide builds up in your body, a sensor in your brain gets very nervous and forces you to take a breath. The hard part of holding your breath isn't lack of oxygen — it's too much carbon dioxide. Trained divers can ignore the signal for a while, but most of us can't.
stories.fun: 海豚和鲸鱼能屏住呼吸潜水好几分钟，因为它们的血液里能多储存很多氧气。

stories.title_zh: 为什么我们会打喷嚏？
stories.title_en: Why do we sneeze?
stories.answer_zh: 鼻子里有非常敏感的小毛和神经，当灰尘、花粉、辣椒、太亮的光等刺激到它们时，大脑就启动一个超快速的反射：把一大口气在很短时间内从鼻子和嘴喷出来，把刺激物冲走。喷嚏的风速能达到每小时一百多公里。
stories.answer_en: Your nose is packed with sensitive hairs and nerves. When dust, pollen, chilli or even bright light tickles them, your brain triggers a lightning-fast reflex that fires a big puff of air out of your nose and mouth to clear the irritant. The wind in a sneeze can travel over a hundred kilometres an hour.
stories.fun: 打喷嚏时几乎不可能睁着眼睛，因为大脑会自动关上眼皮。

stories.title_zh: 感冒为什么会咳嗽？
stories.title_en: Why do we cough when we have a cold?
stories.answer_zh: 感冒病毒在喉咙和气管里引起炎症，让那里黏黏的液体（黏液）增多，还会刺激神经。咳嗽是身体的清理机制：用一阵强劲的气流把黏液和病毒一起冲出来，保护气管和肺。
stories.answer_en: A cold virus inflames your throat and airways, making them produce extra sticky mucus and irritating the nerves. Coughing is the body's cleaning trick — a burst of air that shoots the mucus and germs out, protecting your airways and lungs.
stories.fun: 咳嗽时空气流速比正常呼吸快好几倍，差不多有列车进站时的风。

stories.title_zh: 高山上为什么会喘不过气？
stories.title_en: Why is it hard to breathe high in the mountains?
stories.answer_zh: 海拔越高，空气越稀薄，同样吸一口气，里面的氧气就少了。身体一时不适应，就会觉得呼吸急促、心跳加快、有点头晕。住上几天后，身体会多造一些红细胞来运氧，就能慢慢适应。
stories.answer_en: The higher you climb, the thinner the air. Each breath carries less oxygen, so your body has to work harder. You may feel short of breath, your heart races, and you may get dizzy. After a few days your body makes more red blood cells to carry oxygen and you adapt.
stories.fun: 高原上的运动员训练几周后回到平地，会觉得跑步轻松很多。

stories.title_zh: 感冒和流感有什么不同？
stories.title_en: What's the difference between a cold and the flu?
stories.answer_zh: 感冒和流感都是病毒引起的，但流感的病毒更厉害，所以症状更重：高烧、全身酸痛、特别累。普通感冒可能只是流鼻涕、嗓子痒，几天就好了。流感严重时需要看医生，每年还可以打疫苗预防。
stories.answer_en: Colds and flu are both caused by viruses, but flu viruses hit much harder. Flu often means a high fever, body aches and feeling wiped out. A regular cold might just be a runny nose and scratchy throat that clears in a few days. Bad flu needs a doctor, and many people get a flu shot each year.
stories.fun: 流感病毒每年都会稍微变样，所以疫苗也每年更新一次。

stories.title_zh: 为什么受伤了血会自己止住？
stories.title_en: Why does a cut stop bleeding on its own?
stories.answer_zh: 血液里有一种小细胞叫血小板。一旦伤口出血，血小板会迅速聚集到那里，互相粘成网，再加上一种叫纤维蛋白的丝把网织得更结实，慢慢地形成血块，把伤口堵上。等皮肤长好了，血块会变成痂掉下来。
stories.answer_en: Your blood carries tiny cells called platelets. The moment you bleed, they race to the wound, clump together and form a sticky net. Threads called fibrin make the net stronger, and a clot seals the cut. When new skin grows underneath, the clot dries into a scab and falls off.
stories.fun: 血友病的人体内缺少帮助形成血块的物质，所以小伤口也很难止血。

stories.title_zh: 为什么妈妈摸额头能知道我发烧？
stories.title_en: Why can mum tell I have a fever by touching my forehead?
stories.answer_zh: 正常体温大约三十六到三十七度，跟手掌摸起来差不多温热。如果发烧到三十八九度，皮肤温度明显比平时高，妈妈用手或嘴唇贴一贴就能感觉出来。当然，要确定多少度还得用体温计。
stories.answer_en: Normal body temperature sits around 36-37°C, which feels just slightly warm to the hand. With a fever of 38-39°C the skin is noticeably hotter than usual, so mum can feel it with her palm or lips. To know the exact number, though, you still need a thermometer.
stories.fun: 嘴唇比手掌更敏感，所以妈妈有时候会用嘴唇试温。

stories.title_zh: 感冒为什么要多喝水？
stories.title_en: Why should we drink lots of water with a cold?
stories.answer_zh: 感冒时身体需要造大量黏液去对抗病毒，鼻涕、痰都需要水。发烧时身体出汗、呼吸快，也会流失水分。多喝水能让黏液稀一点容易排出，也能帮身体快点恢复。
stories.answer_en: When you're sick, your body makes lots of mucus to fight germs, and that mucus is mostly water. Fever, sweating and fast breathing all lose water too. Drinking plenty thins the mucus so you can clear it more easily and helps your body bounce back faster.
stories.fun: 热汤、柠檬蜂蜜水之所以让人觉得舒服，不仅暖喉咙，也是在补水。

stories.title_zh: 皮肤为什么会出汗？
stories.title_en: Why do we sweat?
stories.answer_zh: 我们运动或天气热时，身体温度会升高。皮肤里有数百万个汗腺，会把水分送到皮肤表面变成汗。汗在风里蒸发时会带走热量，于是身体就凉快下来。所以出汗其实是身体自己装的"空调"。
stories.answer_en: When you exercise or it's hot, your body warms up. Skin contains millions of tiny sweat glands that send water to the surface. As that sweat evaporates in the breeze, it carries heat away and cools you down. Sweating is your body's built-in air conditioning.
stories.fun: 狗几乎不出汗，所以它们靠张嘴吐舌头喘气来降温。

stories.title_zh: 晒太阳为什么皮肤会变黑？
stories.title_en: Why does our skin turn darker in the sun?
stories.answer_zh: 皮肤里有一种叫黑色素的色素，它能挡住一部分会伤害皮肤的紫外线。晒太阳时，皮肤为了保护自己，就让黑色素细胞多干活，颜色就变深了。所以"变黑"其实是皮肤在自我防晒。
stories.answer_en: Your skin contains a pigment called melanin that blocks some of the sun's damaging ultraviolet rays. When you sit in the sun, your skin tells its melanin cells to work harder, and the colour darkens. The tan is really your skin's own sunscreen.
stories.fun: 防晒霜能直接挡掉一部分紫外线，让黑色素细胞不那么辛苦。

stories.title_zh: 为什么有些人有雀斑？
stories.title_en: Why do some people have freckles?
stories.answer_zh: 雀斑是皮肤里的黑色素分布不太均匀造成的小斑点。有这种情况的人通常皮肤比较白，黑色素细胞被太阳一刺激，会集中在某些地方多产黑色素。冬天阳光少了，雀斑往往会变淡。
stories.answer_en: Freckles are little patches where melanin clumps unevenly in the skin. People who freckle tend to have lighter skin, and their melanin cells respond to sunlight by piling up in spots. In winter, with less sun, freckles often fade.
stories.fun: 雀斑很多时候是家族里一代代传下来的，所以兄弟姐妹常常都有。

stories.title_zh: 为什么伤口结痂会很痒？
stories.title_en: Why does a healing scab itch?
stories.answer_zh: 伤口好起来的时候，皮肤底下在拼命修复，新长出来的神经一开始非常敏感。痂边缘也会拉扯到附近的神经。这些都让大脑收到"痒"的信号，提醒你别去抓，让伤口继续长好。
stories.answer_en: While a cut heals, new skin and nerves grow underneath, and the fresh nerves are extra sensitive. The edges of the scab also tug on tiny nerves nearby. Your brain reads all this as 'itch', a hint to stop scratching so the wound can finish healing.
stories.fun: 抓掉痂会让伤口愈合变慢，还容易留疤。

stories.title_zh: 为什么手指泡水久了会起皱？
stories.title_en: Why do our fingers wrinkle in water?
stories.answer_zh: 手指和脚趾的皮肤有一种特殊的反应：在水里泡久了，神经会让皮下小血管轻微收缩，使皮肤起皱。这种皱可能是进化来的，能让我们抓湿的东西时不打滑，就像汽车轮胎的花纹。
stories.answer_en: Skin on your fingers and toes does something special after a long soak: nerves make tiny vessels under the skin shrink, which causes the wrinkles. Some scientists think we evolved this so we could grip wet things better — a bit like the tread on a tyre.
stories.fun: 脚底也会起皱，但因为我们看不到，常常被忽略。

stories.title_zh: 头发为什么会长？
stories.title_en: Why does hair grow?
stories.answer_zh: 每根头发都是从皮肤里一个叫毛囊的小袋子里长出来的。毛囊底部的细胞不停地分裂，把新细胞往上推，旧细胞死后变硬，就成了头发。我们看到的头发其实都是已经"死掉"的，所以剪它不疼。
stories.answer_en: Each hair grows out of a little pocket in the skin called a follicle. Cells at the bottom of the follicle keep dividing and pushing new cells upward. Older cells die and harden, and that's what becomes the hair you see. Hair is already dead by the time it shows, which is why cutting it doesn't hurt.
stories.fun: 头发一年大约能长十几厘米，全身的毛发大概有十万根头发那么多。

stories.title_zh: 指甲为什么会一直长？
stories.title_en: Why do nails keep growing?
stories.answer_zh: 指甲根部有一片叫"甲母质"的小区域，细胞不停制造新组织，把指甲一点一点往前推。指甲长得慢，平均一周大约一两毫米。有意思的是，常用的手指指甲，比如食指中指，会比小指长得稍微快一点。
stories.answer_en: At the base of your nail there's an area called the matrix where cells keep making new nail and pushing it forward. Nails grow slowly — about one or two millimetres a week. Interestingly, the nails on fingers you use most, like your index and middle fingers, grow a bit faster than your pinkie.
stories.fun: 脚指甲长得比手指甲慢得多，所以剪脚指甲不必那么勤。

stories.title_zh: 鸡皮疙瘩是怎么回事？
stories.title_en: What are goose bumps?
stories.answer_zh: 皮肤上每根细毛的根部都连着一块小小的肌肉。受冷或受惊时，这些小肌肉一起收缩，让细毛立起来，皮肤就鼓出小颗粒，这就是鸡皮疙瘩。对长着浓密毛发的动物来说，毛立起来可以保暖或显得更大；对我们来说，只剩下这个小小的反应。
stories.answer_en: Each tiny hair on your skin has a little muscle at its root. When you're cold or startled, the muscles squeeze and the hairs stand up, leaving small bumps — goose bumps. For furry animals, raised fur traps heat or makes them look bigger. We humans are left with just the bumps.
stories.fun: 听到震撼人心的音乐时，很多人也会起鸡皮疙瘩，这是大脑情绪的反应。

stories.title_zh: 为什么有的人皮肤白，有的人皮肤黑？
stories.title_en: Why do people have different skin colours?
stories.answer_zh: 肤色主要由黑色素决定。祖祖辈辈生活在阳光强烈的地方的人，皮肤里有更多黑色素，可以抵抗紫外线；生活在阳光弱的地方的人，黑色素少，更容易吸收阳光来制造维生素D。所以肤色是人类在不同地方适应环境的结果。
stories.answer_en: Skin colour mostly depends on melanin. People whose ancestors lived under strong sun developed more melanin to block ultraviolet light. People from places with weaker sun made less melanin so their skin could soak up sunlight to make vitamin D. Skin colour is how humans adapted to different parts of the world.
stories.fun: 同一个家族里，兄弟姐妹的肤色有时候也会有细微差别。

stories.title_zh: 为什么有的小朋友头发是卷的？
stories.title_en: Why is some kids' hair curly?
stories.answer_zh: 毛发的形状由毛囊的形状决定。如果毛囊是圆的，头发就长得直；如果毛囊是椭圆甚至扁的，头发就会卷起来。这通常是父母遗传给你的，所以你看一家人，头发常常很像。
stories.answer_en: The shape of a hair depends on the shape of its follicle. Round follicles grow straight hair; oval or flatter follicles make hair curl. The shape is usually passed down from parents, which is why families often have similar hair.
stories.fun: 一根头发能承受大约一百克的重量，所以一整头头发理论上能拉起几辆小汽车。

stories.title_zh: 晒伤之后皮肤为什么会脱皮？
stories.title_en: Why does sunburnt skin peel?
stories.answer_zh: 晒伤是阳光中的紫外线把皮肤细胞晒坏了。身体觉得这些细胞已经不能用了，就把它们丢掉，让下面健康的新细胞顶上来。所以脱皮其实是皮肤在"换新"。
stories.answer_en: Sunburn happens when strong UV light damages skin cells. Your body decides those cells are no longer useful and lets them fall away while fresh, healthy cells take their place. The peeling is really your skin renewing itself.
stories.fun: 晒伤次数太多会增加成年后患皮肤病的风险，所以小朋友要好好涂防晒霜。

stories.title_zh: 为什么洗完澡身体感觉冷？
stories.title_en: Why do we feel cold right after a bath?
stories.answer_zh: 刚出浴时，皮肤上还有一层水。水蒸发时要带走皮肤上的热量，所以你会觉得冷。赶紧用毛巾擦干，水变少了，蒸发也少了，就不那么冷了。
stories.answer_en: Just after stepping out of the bath, your skin is still wet. As the water evaporates, it carries heat away from your body, which makes you feel chilly. Drying off quickly with a towel reduces the water and stops the cooling.
stories.fun: 夏天出汗后吹电风扇会觉得凉快，也是同样的原理。

stories.title_zh: 大脑到底是怎么思考的？
stories.title_en: How does the brain actually think?
stories.answer_zh: 大脑里有差不多八百到一千亿个叫"神经元"的小细胞，它们之间用很短的电信号互相说话。你看到、听到、记住的每一件事，都对应着无数神经元一起放电、组成的小网络。思考就是这些网络在不停变化、重新连接。
stories.answer_en: Your brain holds roughly eighty to a hundred billion cells called neurons that talk to each other with quick electric signals. Every sight, sound or memory matches a network of neurons firing together. Thinking is those networks shifting and rewiring all the time.
stories.fun: 如果把一个大脑的所有神经连接拉成一条线，足够绕地球好几圈。

stories.title_zh: 为什么我们会忘事？
stories.title_en: Why do we forget things?
stories.answer_zh: 大脑每天接收的信息太多了，没法全部留下来。它会自动判断哪些是重要的，反复用的，就保留得久；不重要、很少用的就慢慢淡掉。所以复习背过的单词，是在告诉大脑"这个真的有用"。
stories.answer_en: Your brain takes in so much information each day that it can't keep all of it. It quietly decides what matters — usually the things you use often — and lets less important stuff fade. Reviewing words you've learned tells the brain 'this really matters, please keep it'.
stories.fun: 睡觉时大脑会重新整理记忆，所以学完新东西好好睡一觉特别重要。

stories.title_zh: 情绪是从哪里来的？
stories.title_en: Where do feelings come from?
stories.answer_zh: 大脑里有一块叫"杏仁核"的小区域，专门处理害怕、生气等强烈情绪。其他几个区域则负责高兴、惊讶、悲伤。这些区域综合判断当前发生的事，再决定让你的心跳变快、皮肤变红，或者眼泪掉下来。
stories.answer_en: Deep in your brain a small area called the amygdala handles strong feelings like fear and anger. Other parts deal with joy, surprise and sadness. Together they read what's happening around you and decide whether your heart should race, your face turn red, or tears slide out.
stories.fun: 深呼吸能让杏仁核冷静一些，所以发脾气时数十下呼吸真的有效。

stories.title_zh: 为什么我们害怕黑？
stories.title_en: Why are we afraid of the dark?
stories.answer_zh: 黑暗中我们看不清周围有什么，大脑没办法确定有没有危险，就会保持警惕。古代人晚上面对很多危险动物，会害怕的人活得更久，所以这种警觉就被一代一代传了下来。等灯亮起来，大脑确认安全，害怕就消失了。
stories.answer_en: In the dark, you can't see what's around you, so your brain can't be sure if anything is dangerous. It stays alert. Long ago, our ancestors who were a bit nervous at night survived more often, so the wiring was passed down. Once the light comes on and the brain checks the room, the fear melts away.
stories.fun: 其实在黑暗中，大脑反而会让听觉更灵敏一些。

stories.title_zh: 为什么我们会笑？
stories.title_en: Why do we laugh?
stories.answer_zh: 听到好笑的话或者被挠痒，大脑会释放一种让人开心的化学物质叫"内啡肽"，并启动一连串的肌肉反应：横膈膜跳一跳，让你发出哈哈声。笑还能拉近人与人的距离，是一种社交信号。
stories.answer_en: A joke or a tickle tells your brain to release a feel-good chemical called endorphin and triggers muscle bursts — your diaphragm jumps and out come the 'ha-ha' sounds. Laughter also brings people closer; it's a kind of social glue.
stories.fun: 婴儿大概三四个月就会笑出声音，比说话早多了。

stories.title_zh: 为什么哭完会感觉好一些？
stories.title_en: Why do we feel better after a good cry?
stories.answer_zh: 哭出来时，身体会释放一些缓解疼痛和压力的小化学物质。眼泪里也带走了一些和压力有关的物质。再加上哭出来可以让别人注意到你的难过，得到安慰，所以哭完心情常常会平静很多。
stories.answer_en: Crying releases small chemicals that ease pain and stress. Tears even carry away some stress-related substances. Crying also lets people around you know you need comfort, and once you get it your mood usually settles down.
stories.fun: 有研究发现，难过流出的眼泪和切洋葱时的眼泪化学成分还略有不同。

stories.title_zh: 脑袋是怎么记住电话号码的？
stories.title_en: How does our brain remember a phone number?
stories.answer_zh: 听到一个新号码时，先进入"短期记忆"，只能保存几十秒。如果你重复几次，或者把数字分成有意义的小段（比如把六位数字分成三组两位），大脑就会把它送进长期记忆，存得更久。
stories.answer_en: A new phone number first lands in short-term memory, which lasts only seconds. If you say it a few times or break it into chunks (like splitting six digits into three pairs), your brain pushes it into long-term memory where it can stay much longer.
stories.fun: 魔术师能背圆周率几千位，靠的就是把数字编成有故事的小段。

stories.title_zh: 为什么我们一紧张就会脸红？
stories.title_en: Why do we blush when nervous?
stories.answer_zh: 情绪紧张或不好意思时，大脑会让一种叫肾上腺素的信号增多，让脸部的小血管放大，血流多了，脸就变红了。脸红其实是一种"诚实的信号"：身边的人能看出来你紧张，反而容易包容你。
stories.answer_en: When you're nervous or embarrassed, your brain releases adrenaline that widens the tiny blood vessels in your face. More blood flows and your cheeks turn red. Blushing is an 'honest signal' — people around you can see you're feeling shy, which often makes them go easier on you.
stories.fun: 其实只有人类才会因为情绪而脸红。

stories.title_zh: 为什么记忆有时候会突然涌出来？
stories.title_en: Why do memories sometimes pop up suddenly?
stories.answer_zh: 一种气味、一段旋律、甚至一阵风，都可能成为"钥匙"，打开一段藏在大脑里的记忆。这是因为这些感觉和当时的画面、情绪一起被存下来了。所以闻到外婆家的味道，会突然想起小时候。
stories.answer_en: A smell, a tune or even a breeze can act like a key, unlocking a memory tucked away in your brain. That's because those senses were stored together with the original scene and feelings. So a whiff of grandma's kitchen can pull you straight back to childhood.
stories.fun: 气味比图像更容易勾起情绪记忆，这叫"普鲁斯特效应"。

stories.title_zh: 为什么我们做错事会脸红心跳？
stories.title_en: Why does doing something wrong make our heart pound?
stories.answer_zh: 做错事时，大脑会发出"危险"信号，肾上腺素增多，心跳变快、手心冒汗、脸红。这种身体反应在远古时是为了让我们准备逃跑或道歉。现在虽然没人追，但身体还是按老办法反应。
stories.answer_en: When you do something wrong, your brain sounds an internal 'danger' alarm. Adrenaline rises, your heart speeds up, your palms sweat and your face flushes. Long ago, this prepared us to run away or say sorry quickly. Even though no one's chasing us now, the body still uses the old trick.
stories.fun: 测谎仪其实就是在测心跳、出汗这些紧张反应。

stories.title_zh: 为什么我们玩游戏停不下来？
stories.title_en: Why is it so hard to stop playing a game?
stories.answer_zh: 游戏不断给你奖励：升级、得分、解锁新皮肤。每次得到奖励，大脑会分泌一种叫多巴胺的物质，让你觉得开心，还想继续。如果不主动设个时间限制，多巴胺就让你一关接一关地玩下去。
stories.answer_en: Games keep handing out rewards: levels, points, new skins. Each reward triggers a chemical called dopamine in your brain that makes you feel good and want more. Without setting your own time limit, the dopamine keeps you going round after round.
stories.fun: 看短视频也是同样的机制，所以容易越刷越久。

stories.title_zh: 为什么有时候话到嘴边却想不起来？
stories.title_en: Why do words sometimes get stuck on the tip of our tongue?
stories.answer_zh: 大脑找词时，要在巨大的"词库"里搜索。如果某个词的连接暂时找不到，明明知道意思却说不出来。专家叫这种现象"舌尖现象"。通常停下来想一想，或者听到一两个提示，词就会突然蹦出来。
stories.answer_en: When your brain looks for a word, it searches through a huge inner dictionary. Sometimes the connection to a specific word is briefly hard to find, even though you know what it means. Experts call this 'tip of the tongue'. Pausing or hearing a small clue often lets the word jump back.
stories.fun: 这种现象在多种语言之间切换的人身上特别常见。

categories.title: 厨房里的科学
categories.subtitle: 锅里、冰箱里、杯子里每天都在做实验

stories.title_zh: 为什么虾一炒就变红？
stories.title_en: Why do shrimp turn red when cooked?
stories.answer_zh: 生虾壳里有一种红色素，平时被蛋白质包着，看起来灰灰的。加热后蛋白质形状变了，红色素露出来，所以虾就像换了衣服一样变红。
stories.answer_en: Raw shrimp shells contain a red pigment that is hidden by proteins. Heat changes the proteins, so the red pigment shows through and the shrimp turns red.
stories.fun: 螃蟹和龙虾煮熟变红，也是同一类变化。

stories.title_zh: 为什么鸡蛋煮熟会变硬？
stories.title_en: Why does an egg become hard when boiled?
stories.answer_zh: 鸡蛋里的蛋白质原来像松散的线团，受热后会展开并互相拉住，形成一张小网。水被网困住，流动的蛋液就变成了软软的固体。
stories.answer_en: Egg proteins start like loose threads. Heat unfolds them and makes them link together into a net, trapping water and turning runny egg into a soft solid.
stories.fun: 煎蛋边缘脆、中间嫩，是因为受热程度不同。

stories.title_zh: 为什么烤面包会有香味？
stories.title_en: Why does toast smell so good?
stories.answer_zh: 面包表面的糖和蛋白质遇到高温，会发生一种叫美拉德反应的变化，产生棕色外皮和许多香味分子。它不是简单“烤干”，而是产生了新味道。
stories.answer_en: On the bread surface, sugars and proteins react in high heat. This Maillard reaction makes brown crust and many aroma molecules, creating new flavour.
stories.fun: 烤肉、薯条、饼干的香味也常来自这个反应。

stories.title_zh: 为什么高压锅能更快炖烂肉？
stories.title_en: Why does a pressure cooker soften meat faster?
stories.answer_zh: 高压锅把水蒸气关在锅里，锅内压力升高，水能比一百度更热还不猛烈沸腾。更高的温度让肉里的胶原蛋白更快变软。
stories.answer_en: A pressure cooker traps steam, raising pressure so water can get hotter than 100°C. The higher temperature softens collagen in meat faster.
stories.fun: 开高压锅前要先放气，因为里面真的存着高压。

stories.title_zh: 为什么煎牛排要先把锅烧热？
stories.title_en: Why heat the pan before searing steak?
stories.answer_zh: 热锅能让肉表面迅速升温，产生香味和漂亮的褐色外壳。如果锅不够热，肉会慢慢出水，像被煮了一样。
stories.answer_en: A hot pan quickly heats the meat surface, making a tasty brown crust. If the pan is not hot enough, the meat leaks water and steams instead.
stories.fun: 煎东西时听见“滋啦”声，常常是水分正在快速变成蒸汽。

stories.title_zh: 为什么蒸东西比烤东西更湿润？
stories.title_en: Why is steamed food moister than baked food?
stories.answer_zh: 蒸锅里充满水蒸气，食物表面不容易变干。烤箱是热空气，水分会不断蒸发，所以表面更容易干和脆。
stories.answer_en: A steamer is full of water vapour, so food surfaces do not dry easily. An oven uses hot air, which evaporates water and makes surfaces drier or crispier.
stories.fun: 馒头靠蒸保持柔软，饼干靠烤变酥。

stories.title_zh: 为什么炒菜要不停翻动？
stories.title_en: Why do we stir-fry while moving the food?
stories.answer_zh: 锅底温度最高，食物如果一直贴着同一个地方会糊。翻动能让每一块轮流接触热锅，也让水汽散开。
stories.answer_en: The pan bottom is hottest, so food can burn if it stays in one spot. Stirring lets pieces take turns touching the hot pan and lets steam escape.
stories.fun: 好厨师的手速，其实是在管理热量。

stories.title_zh: 为什么烤红薯会流出糖浆？
stories.title_en: Why does a roasted sweet potato leak syrup?
stories.answer_zh: 红薯受热时，里面的淀粉会慢慢变成更甜的小糖分子。水分蒸发后糖变浓，就可能从裂口流出来。
stories.answer_en: Heat slowly turns some sweet potato starch into smaller sweet sugars. As water evaporates, the sugar becomes concentrated and may ooze from cracks.
stories.fun: 慢烤的红薯常比急烤更甜。

stories.title_zh: 为什么鱼煎之前要擦干？
stories.title_en: Why dry fish before frying it?
stories.answer_zh: 鱼表面的水碰到热油会先变成水蒸气，阻止表面变脆，还会让油乱溅。擦干后，表面更容易煎香。
stories.answer_en: Water on fish turns to steam in hot oil, stopping browning and causing splatter. Drying the surface helps it crisp and brown.
stories.fun: 厨房纸不是只是擦干净，它有时是在帮忙做口感。

stories.title_zh: 为什么汤煮久了味道更浓？
stories.title_en: Why does soup taste richer after simmering?
stories.answer_zh: 长时间小火能把肉、骨头、蔬菜里的味道分子慢慢溶到水里，同时蒸发掉一部分水，味道就更集中。
stories.answer_en: Slow simmering lets flavour molecules move from meat, bones and vegetables into the water while some water evaporates, concentrating the taste.
stories.fun: 好汤常常不是“大火赢”，而是“时间赢”。

stories.title_zh: 为什么冰会浮在水上？
stories.title_en: Why does ice float on water?
stories.answer_zh: 大多数东西变成固体会更紧密，但水结冰时分子排成比较松的架子，占的空间变大，密度变小，所以冰会浮起来。
stories.answer_en: Most substances pack tighter when solid, but water forms an open structure when it freezes. Ice takes more space and becomes less dense, so it floats.
stories.fun: 如果冰会沉，湖底会先冻住，很多水生生物就难过冬了。

stories.title_zh: 为什么汽水打开会冒泡？
stories.title_en: Why does soda fizz when opened?
stories.answer_zh: 汽水瓶里用压力把二氧化碳压进水中。打开瓶盖后压力突然变小，气体不愿意继续待在水里，就变成泡泡逃出来。
stories.answer_en: Soda holds carbon dioxide under pressure. Opening the bottle lowers the pressure, so the gas leaves the liquid as bubbles.
stories.fun: 摇过汽水后已经有很多小气泡，打开就更容易喷。

stories.title_zh: 为什么冷饮杯外面会出水？
stories.title_en: Why does a cold glass sweat?
stories.answer_zh: 杯子外面的水不是漏出来的。空气里的水蒸气碰到冰冷杯壁后降温，变成小水滴，像空气在杯子上“下了一场小雨”。
stories.answer_en: The water outside the glass did not leak out. Water vapour in the air cools on the cold surface and condenses into droplets.
stories.fun: 浴室镜子起雾，也是水蒸气遇冷凝结。

stories.title_zh: 为什么盐水不容易结冰？
stories.title_en: Why does salt water freeze less easily?
stories.answer_zh: 水结冰需要分子整齐排队。盐溶进去后会打乱排队，水要降到更低温度才能结冰。
stories.answer_en: Water molecules must arrange neatly to freeze. Dissolved salt disrupts the arrangement, so the water must get colder before freezing.
stories.fun: 冬天路上撒盐，就是为了让薄冰更容易融化。

stories.title_zh: 为什么热水会冒透明的水蒸气却看到白雾？
stories.title_en: Why is steam invisible but mist white?
stories.answer_zh: 真正的水蒸气是看不见的。它离开热水后遇冷，凝结成许多小水滴，我们看到的白雾就是这些小水滴。
stories.answer_en: Real water vapour is invisible. When it leaves hot water and cools, it condenses into tiny droplets; those droplets make the white mist.
stories.fun: 云也是许多小水滴或小冰晶聚在一起。

stories.title_zh: 为什么吸管能把饮料吸上来？
stories.title_en: Why can a straw lift a drink?
stories.answer_zh: 你吸走吸管里的空气，里面压力变小。外面的大气压压着杯中液面，把饮料推上吸管。
stories.answer_en: Sucking removes air from the straw and lowers pressure inside it. Higher air pressure outside pushes the drink up the straw.
stories.fun: 其实不是你把饮料“拉”上来，而是空气把它“推”上来。

stories.title_zh: 为什么热水里茶叶颜色出来得快？
stories.title_en: Why does tea colour come out faster in hot water?
stories.answer_zh: 热水分子运动更快，能更快钻进茶叶，把色素和香味分子带出来。冷水也能泡茶，只是需要更久。
stories.answer_en: Hot water molecules move faster and enter tea leaves more quickly, carrying out colour and aroma molecules. Cold water works too, but takes longer.
stories.fun: 冷泡茶味道更柔和，就是因为出来的物质比例不一样。

stories.title_zh: 为什么冰块放进饮料会裂？
stories.title_en: Why do ice cubes crack in a drink?
stories.answer_zh: 冰块外层突然遇到较暖的饮料，外面先膨胀，里面还很冷。内外变化不同步，冰块内部产生裂缝。
stories.answer_en: The outside of an ice cube warms and expands first while the inside remains colder. Uneven change creates cracks inside the ice.
stories.fun: 有时能听见“咔”的一声，就是冰在开裂。

stories.title_zh: 为什么运动饮料有点咸？
stories.title_en: Why do sports drinks taste a little salty?
stories.answer_zh: 流汗时身体不只失去水，也会失去一些盐分。运动饮料里的盐和糖能帮助身体更快吸收水分。
stories.answer_en: Sweat loses not only water but also salts. The salt and sugar in sports drinks help the body absorb water more quickly.
stories.fun: 普通玩耍不一定需要运动饮料，白水通常就够。

stories.title_zh: 为什么可乐冻成冰会把瓶子撑坏？
stories.title_en: Why can frozen cola break its bottle?
stories.answer_zh: 水结冰会膨胀，汽水里还有二氧化碳。封闭瓶子里空间不够时，膨胀的冰和气体压力可能把瓶子撑裂。
stories.answer_en: Water expands when it freezes, and soda also contains carbon dioxide. In a closed bottle, expanding ice and gas pressure can crack it.
stories.fun: 不要把满瓶汽水长时间放进冷冻室。

stories.title_zh: 为什么面团会发起来？
stories.title_en: Why does dough rise?
stories.answer_zh: 酵母会吃面粉里的糖，产生二氧化碳。气体被有弹性的面筋网兜住，面团就慢慢鼓起来。
stories.answer_en: Yeast eats sugars in flour and releases carbon dioxide. The gas is trapped by stretchy gluten, so the dough puffs up.
stories.fun: 水太烫会伤到酵母，面团就难发起来。

stories.title_zh: 为什么酸奶是酸的？
stories.title_en: Why is yogurt sour?
stories.answer_zh: 酸奶里的有益细菌会把牛奶中的乳糖变成乳酸。乳酸带来酸味，也让牛奶蛋白变得更稠，形成酸奶的口感。
stories.answer_en: Helpful bacteria in yogurt turn milk sugar into lactic acid. The acid gives sour flavour and thickens milk proteins into yogurt texture.
stories.fun: 酸奶不是坏牛奶，而是被好细菌认真改造过的牛奶。

stories.title_zh: 为什么食物放久了会发霉？
stories.title_en: Why does food grow mould?
stories.answer_zh: 空气里到处有很小的霉菌孢子。它们落到潮湿、有营养的食物上，就会长出菌丝，慢慢变成我们看见的霉斑。
stories.answer_en: Tiny mould spores float in air. When they land on moist nutritious food, they grow threads that become visible mould patches.
stories.fun: 看见一点霉，里面可能已经有看不见的菌丝。

stories.title_zh: 为什么冰箱不能让食物永远不坏？
stories.title_en: Why can’t a fridge keep food fresh forever?
stories.answer_zh: 低温能让细菌和霉菌长得慢一些，但不能让它们完全停止，也不能把已经产生的坏味道变没。冰箱像慢速键，不是时光机。
stories.answer_en: Cold slows bacteria and mould, but it does not stop them completely or remove bad flavours already formed. A fridge is a slow button, not a time machine.
stories.fun: 剩饭尽快冷藏，是为了不给细菌太多舒服时间。

stories.title_zh: 为什么晒干的水果能放更久？
stories.title_en: Why does dried fruit last longer?
stories.answer_zh: 细菌和霉菌需要水才能舒服地生长。把水果里的水分减少，它们就很难快速繁殖，食物能保存更久。
stories.answer_en: Bacteria and mould need water to grow well. Removing much of the water makes it hard for them to multiply quickly, so fruit lasts longer.
stories.fun: 葡萄干不是小葡萄品种，而是葡萄失水后的样子。

stories.title_zh: 为什么腌菜很咸却能保存？
stories.title_en: Why can salty pickles last longer?
stories.answer_zh: 高盐环境会把微生物细胞里的水“拉”出来，让很多坏菌难以生长。再加上发酵产生的酸，保存效果更强。
stories.answer_en: High salt pulls water out of many microbes, making it hard for them to grow. Acids from fermentation can help preserve food too.
stories.fun: 保存食物常常是在控制水、盐、酸和温度。

stories.title_zh: 为什么米饭放冰箱后会变硬？
stories.title_en: Why does rice harden in the fridge?
stories.answer_zh: 熟米饭冷却后，淀粉分子会慢慢重新排队，水分也移动出来，饭粒就变硬。加一点水再加热可以软回去。
stories.answer_en: As cooked rice cools, starch molecules line up again and water moves out, making grains firm. Adding water and reheating softens them.
stories.fun: 隔夜饭适合炒饭，因为不容易粘成一团。

stories.title_zh: 为什么罐头能保存很久？
stories.title_en: Why can canned food last so long?
stories.answer_zh: 罐头会先加热杀死大多数微生物，再密封起来，不让新的微生物进去。没有捣乱者，食物就能放很久。
stories.answer_en: Canning heats food to kill most microbes and then seals it so new microbes cannot enter. With no spoilers inside, food lasts long.
stories.fun: 罐头鼓起来可能代表里面产气了，不能随便吃。

stories.title_zh: 为什么蜂蜜不容易坏？
stories.title_en: Why does honey rarely spoil?
stories.answer_zh: 蜂蜜含水很少、糖很浓，很多微生物会被“吸干”而无法生长。它还带有一些天然抑菌物质。
stories.answer_en: Honey has little water and lots of sugar, which dries out many microbes and stops them growing. It also has some natural antimicrobial substances.
stories.fun: 蜂蜜结晶不是坏了，只是糖分重新排列。

stories.title_zh: 为什么冷冻能保存食物？
stories.title_en: Why does freezing preserve food?
stories.answer_zh: 低温让微生物活动大大变慢，也让食物里的化学变化慢下来。但冷冻不能杀死所有微生物，解冻后仍要小心。
stories.answer_en: Low temperature greatly slows microbes and chemical changes in food. But freezing does not kill everything, so thawed food still needs care.
stories.fun: 冷冻像按慢动作，不是按删除键。

stories.title_zh: 为什么微波炉能加热饭菜？
stories.title_en: How does a microwave heat food?
stories.answer_zh: 微波会让食物里的水分子快速转动和摆动，分子互相碰撞，食物就热起来。哪里含水多，哪里通常热得更明显。
stories.answer_en: Microwaves make water molecules in food wiggle rapidly. Their collisions warm the food, especially in wetter parts.
stories.fun: 干盘子不太热，湿饭菜很热，就是这个原因。

stories.title_zh: 为什么金属锅把手会烫？
stories.title_en: Why do metal pot handles get hot?
stories.answer_zh: 金属很会传热，锅底的热可以沿着金属一路传到把手。木头和塑料传热慢，所以常用来做隔热手柄。
stories.answer_en: Metal conducts heat well, so heat travels from the pot to the handle. Wood and plastic conduct poorly, so they are used for safer handles.
stories.fun: 同样温度下，金属摸起来更冷或更烫，是因为它传热太快。

stories.title_zh: 为什么保温杯能保温？
stories.title_en: Why does a thermos keep drinks warm?
stories.answer_zh: 保温杯通常有双层壁，中间接近真空，热量很难通过空气传出去。亮亮的内壁还会反射一部分热。
stories.answer_en: A thermos has double walls with near-vacuum between them, so heat cannot travel easily through air. Shiny surfaces reflect some heat too.
stories.fun: 保温杯也能保冷，因为它是在减慢热量交换。

stories.title_zh: 为什么玻璃杯倒热水可能裂？
stories.title_en: Why can glass crack with hot water?
stories.answer_zh: 热水让玻璃内层先膨胀，外层还没来得及热起来，内外膨胀不同步，玻璃内部被拉扯，就可能裂开。
stories.answer_en: Hot water makes the inner glass expand before the outside warms. Uneven expansion creates stress that can crack the glass.
stories.fun: 耐热玻璃的膨胀小，所以更能承受冷热变化。

stories.title_zh: 为什么菜刀越薄越容易切？
stories.title_en: Why does a thinner knife cut better?
stories.answer_zh: 同样的力集中在更小的刀刃面积上，压力就更大，更容易把食物分开。钝刀面积大，反而要用更多力。
stories.answer_en: The same force on a smaller blade edge creates higher pressure, making food easier to split. A dull thick edge needs more force.
stories.fun: 纸边也可能割手，因为边缘很薄。

stories.title_zh: 为什么锅盖上会有水珠？
stories.title_en: Why do droplets form on a pot lid?
stories.answer_zh: 锅里的水蒸气碰到较冷的锅盖，会凝结成小水滴。水滴变大后，就会滴回锅里。
stories.answer_en: Water vapour from the pot meets the cooler lid and condenses into droplets. When droplets grow, they fall back.
stories.fun: 这是一场锅里的小水循环。

stories.title_zh: 为什么不粘锅不能用铁铲乱刮？
stories.title_en: Why not scrape a non-stick pan with metal tools?
stories.answer_zh: 不粘锅表面有一层光滑涂层。铁铲太硬，可能刮伤涂层，食物就更容易粘，也可能影响安全。
stories.answer_en: A non-stick pan has a smooth coating. Hard metal tools can scratch it, making food stick and possibly affecting safety.
stories.fun: 木铲和硅胶铲更温柔。

stories.title_zh: 为什么烤箱要预热？
stories.title_en: Why does an oven need preheating?
stories.answer_zh: 刚打开烤箱时，空气、金属架和内壁都还没到设定温度。预热让整个空间稳定，食物一进去就按正确温度受热。
stories.answer_en: At first, oven air, racks and walls are not at the set temperature. Preheating stabilises the space so food cooks correctly.
stories.fun: 蛋糕特别怕温度乱，因为结构要慢慢定型。

stories.title_zh: 为什么漏勺能捞起食物却漏掉水？
stories.title_en: Why does a slotted spoon hold food but drain water?
stories.answer_zh: 漏勺孔洞比大多数食物小，却比水分子组成的液体通道大。固体被挡住，液体能流走。
stories.answer_en: The holes are smaller than most food pieces but large enough for liquid to flow through. Solids stay; water drains.
stories.fun: 筛子、滤网、茶漏都是利用大小差异分离东西。

stories.title_zh: 为什么擀面杖能把面团擀薄？
stories.title_en: Why can a rolling pin flatten dough?
stories.answer_zh: 擀面杖把手上的力分散到长长的圆柱面上，来回滚动时不断把面团向外推开，厚度就变小。
stories.answer_en: A rolling pin spreads your force along a cylinder. Rolling pushes dough outward again and again, making it thinner.
stories.fun: 它不是压一次成功，而是很多次小变形累积。

stories.title_zh: 为什么辣椒让嘴像着火？
stories.title_en: Why does chilli feel like fire?
stories.answer_zh: 辣椒素会刺激舌头上感受高温的神经，让大脑误以为嘴里很烫。其实温度没升高，是神经被“骗”了。
stories.answer_en: Capsaicin triggers heat-sensing nerves on the tongue, so the brain thinks the mouth is burning even though the temperature has not risen.
stories.fun: 喝水不太解辣，牛奶常更有用，因为辣椒素更喜欢油脂。

stories.title_zh: 为什么薄荷让嘴里发凉？
stories.title_en: Why does mint feel cold?
stories.answer_zh: 薄荷醇会刺激冷觉神经，让大脑收到“凉”的信号。嘴里并没有真的变冷很多，但感觉像开了小空调。
stories.answer_en: Menthol activates cold-sensing nerves. Your mouth is not actually much colder, but your brain receives a cool signal.
stories.fun: 辣椒骗热觉，薄荷骗冷觉。

stories.title_zh: 为什么油锅着火不能泼水？
stories.title_en: Why must we not pour water on burning oil?
stories.answer_zh: 水比油重，会沉到热油下面，瞬间变成大量水蒸气，把燃烧的油滴炸开，火反而更大。
stories.answer_en: Water sinks below hot oil and flashes into steam, throwing burning oil droplets outward and spreading the fire.
stories.fun: 正确做法是关火，用锅盖或灭火毯隔绝空气。

stories.title_zh: 为什么生熟砧板要分开？
stories.title_en: Why separate raw and cooked cutting boards?
stories.answer_zh: 生肉上可能有细菌，做熟能杀掉它们。但如果生肉砧板又切水果，细菌就可能搬到直接入口的食物上。
stories.answer_en: Raw meat can carry bacteria that cooking would kill. If the same board cuts fruit, bacteria can move to food eaten directly.
stories.fun: 厨房卫生最怕的是看不见的微生物搬家。

stories.title_zh: 为什么盐能让甜味更明显？
stories.title_en: Why can salt make sweetness stronger?
stories.answer_zh: 少量盐能压住一点苦味，也能让味蕾更注意到甜味和香味。它像把味道的背景噪音调低。
stories.answer_en: A little salt can reduce bitterness and help taste buds notice sweetness and aroma more clearly, like lowering flavour noise.
stories.fun: 甜点里的一小撮盐不是为了变咸。

stories.title_zh: 为什么香料磨碎后更香？
stories.title_en: Why do ground spices smell stronger?
stories.answer_zh: 香味分子藏在香料细胞和油脂里。磨碎会打破细胞、增加表面积，更多香味跑到空气中。
stories.answer_en: Aroma molecules hide in spice cells and oils. Grinding breaks cells and increases surface area, letting more aroma escape.
stories.fun: 现磨胡椒通常比放久的胡椒粉香。

stories.title_zh: 为什么不能把清洁剂随便混在一起？
stories.title_en: Why shouldn’t cleaners be mixed randomly?
stories.answer_zh: 不同清洁剂里有不同化学物质，混合后可能产生刺激性甚至有毒气体。看起来都能清洁，不代表能一起用。
stories.answer_en: Different cleaners contain different chemicals. Mixing them can create irritating or even poisonous gases. Cleaning ability does not mean compatibility.
stories.fun: 厨房和浴室的科学，第一条永远是安全。

stories.title_zh: 为什么闻到煤气味不能开灯？
stories.title_en: Why not turn on lights if you smell gas?
stories.answer_zh: 煤气和空气混合到一定程度，遇到小火花可能燃烧。电灯开关有时会产生极小火花，所以要先开窗、关阀、离开。
stories.answer_en: Gas mixed with air can ignite from a tiny spark. Light switches can make small sparks, so open windows, shut the valve and leave.
stories.fun: 煤气的臭味是人为加进去的警告味。

stories.title_zh: 为什么砧板用完要晾干？
stories.title_en: Why dry a cutting board after washing?
stories.answer_zh: 潮湿表面适合细菌和霉菌生长。洗干净后晾干，等于把它们喜欢的水分拿走。
stories.answer_en: A wet surface helps bacteria and mould grow. Drying the board removes the water they like.
stories.fun: 干燥是厨房卫生的好朋友。

stories.title_zh: 为什么热汤不能马上放进薄玻璃杯？
stories.title_en: Why not pour hot soup into thin glass?
stories.answer_zh: 薄玻璃遇热时内外温差变化很快，膨胀不均匀，可能开裂。耐热容器能更好承受这种变化。
stories.answer_en: Thin glass can heat unevenly and expand unevenly, causing cracks. Heat-resistant containers handle the change better.
stories.fun: 看容器标识，比靠运气安全。

categories.title: 家里的物理
categories.subtitle: 电、光、声音、冷热和运动都藏在家里

stories.title_zh: 电到底是什么？
stories.title_en: What is electricity?
stories.answer_zh: 电可以理解成带电粒子的运动和它们之间的推动。家里的电线里有许多电子，电源像泵一样推动它们，让灯、手机和电机工作。
stories.answer_en: Electricity is the movement and pushing of charged particles. In wires, many electrons are pushed by a power source so lamps, phones and motors can work.
stories.fun: 电不是一团看得见的东西，更像一队被指挥的微小粒子。

stories.title_zh: 为什么电灯一按就亮？
stories.title_en: Why does a light turn on instantly?
stories.answer_zh: 开关闭合后，电路变成完整通道，电场几乎立刻传遍导线，推动整条导线里的电子一起动。
stories.answer_en: When the switch closes, the circuit becomes a complete path. The electric field spreads almost instantly and pushes electrons throughout the wire.
stories.fun: 不是一个电子从开关跑到灯泡，而是整条队伍同时收到命令。

stories.title_zh: 为什么电线外面要包塑料？
stories.title_en: Why are wires covered in plastic?
stories.answer_zh: 金属导电，塑料不容易导电。塑料外皮让电流待在金属里，也保护人的手不碰到带电部分。
stories.answer_en: Metal conducts electricity; plastic does not conduct well. The plastic jacket keeps current inside the metal and protects our hands.
stories.fun: 破皮电线很危险，因为保护层不完整了。

stories.title_zh: 为什么静电会“啪”一下？
stories.title_en: Why does static electricity snap?
stories.answer_zh: 衣服摩擦时，电子会从一个物体跑到另一个物体，电荷暂时堆积。碰到金属时电荷突然流走，就产生小电击。
stories.answer_en: Rubbing moves electrons from one surface to another, building up charge. Touching metal lets the charge rush away as a tiny shock.
stories.fun: 冬天空气干，电荷不容易散，静电更明显。

stories.title_zh: 为什么电池有正负极？
stories.title_en: Why does a battery have positive and negative ends?
stories.answer_zh: 电池内部的化学反应会把电荷分开，让一端更愿意给电子，另一端更愿意收电子。接成回路后，电子就有方向地流动。
stories.answer_en: Chemical reactions inside a battery separate charge, making one end give electrons more easily and the other receive them. A circuit lets electrons flow.
stories.fun: 电池像一个小小的化学电泵。

stories.title_zh: 为什么手机充电器会发热？
stories.title_en: Why does a phone charger get warm?
stories.answer_zh: 充电器要把插座里的电变成手机能用的形式。转换过程中不可能百分百完美，一部分能量变成热。
stories.answer_en: A charger changes wall electricity into a form a phone can use. Conversion is not perfect, and some energy becomes heat.
stories.fun: 温温的正常，烫手就要停止使用。

stories.title_zh: 为什么不能把太多电器插在一个插排上？
stories.title_en: Why not plug too many devices into one power strip?
stories.answer_zh: 电线能安全承受的电流有限。太多大功率电器同时工作，电线会发热，严重时可能起火。
stories.answer_en: Wires have safe current limits. Too many high-power devices can heat the wires and may cause fire.
stories.fun: 热水壶、吹风机、电暖器都很吃电。

stories.title_zh: 为什么保险丝会烧断？
stories.title_en: Why does a fuse blow?
stories.answer_zh: 电流太大时，保险丝里的细金属先发热熔断，把电路断开，防止后面的电线继续过热。
stories.answer_en: When current is too high, the thin metal in a fuse heats and melts, opening the circuit before wires overheat.
stories.fun: 它是用牺牲自己来保护电路。

stories.title_zh: 为什么LED灯比老灯泡省电？
stories.title_en: Why are LED bulbs more efficient?
stories.answer_zh: 老灯泡把金属丝烧到发光，很多电能变成热。LED让半导体直接发光，浪费成热的能量少。
stories.answer_en: Old bulbs heat a filament until it glows, wasting much energy as heat. LEDs make semiconductors emit light directly.
stories.fun: 老灯泡很烫，就是能量跑去当热了。

stories.title_zh: 为什么湿手不能碰插座？
stories.title_en: Why shouldn’t wet hands touch sockets?
stories.answer_zh: 生活中的水常含有盐分，能导电。湿手会降低皮肤电阻，让电流更容易通过身体，非常危险。
stories.answer_en: Everyday water often contains salts and conducts electricity. Wet skin has lower resistance, making current pass through the body more easily.
stories.fun: 洗完手先擦干，再碰电器。

stories.title_zh: 为什么镜子能照出人？
stories.title_en: Why can a mirror show your face?
stories.answer_zh: 镜子表面非常光滑，光线会按整齐方向反射回来。你脸上的光经过镜子反进眼睛，大脑就看到镜中的你。
stories.answer_en: A mirror is very smooth, so light reflects in an orderly direction. Light from your face bounces into your eyes, and your brain sees your image.
stories.fun: 墙也反光，只是反得太乱，所以没有清楚影像。

stories.title_zh: 为什么影子会变长变短？
stories.title_en: Why do shadows change length?
stories.answer_zh: 影子是物体挡住光形成的。光源越低，光线越斜，影子越长；太阳越接近头顶，影子越短。
stories.answer_en: A shadow forms where an object blocks light. A low light source gives slanted light and long shadows; overhead sunlight makes short shadows.
stories.fun: 早晚影子长，中午影子短。

stories.title_zh: 为什么彩虹有很多颜色？
stories.title_en: Why does a rainbow have many colours?
stories.answer_zh: 阳光看起来白，其实混合了很多颜色。雨滴像小棱镜，把不同颜色折弯到不同方向，于是颜色分开了。
stories.answer_en: White sunlight is a mix of many colours. Raindrops act like tiny prisms, bending different colours by different amounts.
stories.fun: 彩虹不是挂在某个地方，而是你、太阳和雨滴的位置配合出来的。

stories.title_zh: 为什么水里的筷子像弯了？
stories.title_en: Why does a chopstick look bent in water?
stories.answer_zh: 光从水进入空气时速度改变，方向也会折弯。眼睛以为光一直直走，所以水下那段看起来被挪开了。
stories.answer_en: Light changes speed and direction when it moves from water into air. Your eyes assume light came straight, so the underwater part looks shifted.
stories.fun: 游泳池看起来比实际浅，也有折射的原因。

stories.title_zh: 为什么白衣服夏天更凉快？
stories.title_en: Why are white clothes cooler in summer?
stories.answer_zh: 白色会反射较多阳光，吸收的能量少。黑色吸收更多光并变成热，所以在太阳下更热。
stories.answer_en: White reflects more sunlight and absorbs less energy. Black absorbs more light and turns it into heat.
stories.fun: 车内深色座椅晒过后也会更烫。

stories.title_zh: 为什么晚上窗户像镜子？
stories.title_en: Why do windows look like mirrors at night?
stories.answer_zh: 玻璃既透光也反光。晚上屋里亮、外面暗，屋内反射光比外面进来的光强，所以看见倒影。
stories.answer_en: Glass both transmits and reflects light. At night, indoor reflected light is stronger than dim outdoor light, so you see a reflection.
stories.fun: 白天外面亮，窗户就更像透明的。

stories.title_zh: 为什么相机要对焦？
stories.title_en: Why does a camera need focus?
stories.answer_zh: 镜头要把来自某个距离的光准确汇聚到感光元件上。焦点不对，光点散开，照片就模糊。
stories.answer_en: A lens must bring light from a certain distance onto the sensor. If focus is wrong, light spreads and the picture blurs.
stories.fun: 眼睛里的晶状体也在不断对焦。

stories.title_zh: 为什么太阳镜能防刺眼？
stories.title_en: Why do sunglasses reduce glare?
stories.answer_zh: 太阳镜吸收或反射一部分强光。偏光镜还能挡掉水面、路面反射来的某些方向的光。
stories.answer_en: Sunglasses absorb or reflect some bright light. Polarised lenses also block certain reflected glare from roads or water.
stories.fun: 钓鱼的人喜欢偏光镜，因为能看清水面下。

stories.title_zh: 为什么光比声音快？
stories.title_en: Why is light faster than sound?
stories.answer_zh: 光是电磁波，在空气里速度极快；声音要靠空气分子一个推一个传振动，所以慢得多。
stories.answer_en: Light is an electromagnetic wave and travels extremely fast; sound must pass vibration from molecule to molecule, so it is much slower.
stories.fun: 所以雷雨时先看见闪电，后听见雷。

stories.title_zh: 为什么激光笔不能照眼睛？
stories.title_en: Why shouldn’t lasers shine into eyes?
stories.answer_zh: 激光很集中，进入眼睛后又会被晶状体聚焦到视网膜上，可能把小区域照伤。
stories.answer_en: Laser light is concentrated, and the eye lens focuses it onto the retina, where it can damage a tiny area.
stories.fun: 看起来小小一束光，也可能很危险。

stories.title_zh: 声音是怎么跑到耳朵里的？
stories.title_en: How does sound reach our ears?
stories.answer_zh: 声音来自振动。物体振动推动空气，空气一层推一层形成声波，传到耳朵后让耳膜振动。
stories.answer_en: Sound comes from vibration. A vibrating object pushes air into waves, which reach the ear and vibrate the eardrum.
stories.fun: 没有空气的太空里，声音很难传播。

stories.title_zh: 为什么空房间回声大？
stories.title_en: Why does an empty room echo?
stories.answer_zh: 硬墙会反射声音。空房间里缺少窗帘、沙发、地毯这些吸音的软东西，声音反射得多，就有明显回声。
stories.answer_en: Hard walls reflect sound. Empty rooms lack soft curtains, sofas and carpets that absorb sound, so echoes are stronger.
stories.fun: 录音棚墙上常有软材料，就是为了吸收反射声。

stories.title_zh: 为什么录音里的自己不像自己？
stories.title_en: Why does your recorded voice sound strange?
stories.answer_zh: 平时听自己说话，一部分声音通过头骨传到内耳，低音更多。录音只记录空气里的声音，所以听起来更陌生。
stories.answer_en: When you speak, some sound reaches your inner ear through your skull, adding low tones. A recording only captures sound through air.
stories.fun: 别人平时听到的你，更接近录音里的声音。

stories.title_zh: 为什么蚊子的声音特别烦？
stories.title_en: Why is mosquito buzzing so annoying?
stories.answer_zh: 蚊子翅膀快速拍动，发出细高的声音。它又常在耳边出现，大脑会把这种声音当成“有东西靠近皮肤”的警报。
stories.answer_en: Mosquito wings beat fast and make a thin high buzz near the ear. The brain treats it as an alert that something is close to the skin.
stories.fun: 听见蚊子声，人会自动开始找它。

stories.title_zh: 为什么低音会震胸口？
stories.title_en: Why can bass shake your chest?
stories.answer_zh: 低音频率低、波长长，常推动较多空气。身体某些部位会跟着轻微振动，所以你不只是听到，还能感觉到。
stories.answer_en: Bass has low frequency and long wavelength, often moving lots of air. Parts of the body vibrate with it, so you feel it.
stories.fun: 演唱会音箱前的震动，就是声音能量在身体上留下感觉。

stories.title_zh: 为什么隔着门还能听见声音？
stories.title_en: Why can sound pass through a door?
stories.answer_zh: 声音让空气振动，也能让门板微微振动。门板再把振动传给另一边空气，所以声音会变小但还能过去。
stories.answer_en: Sound vibrates air and can make the door vibrate. The door passes vibration to air on the other side, so sound gets through but quieter.
stories.fun: 厚重门比薄门隔音好。

stories.title_zh: 为什么耳机有左声道右声道？
stories.title_en: Why do headphones have left and right channels?
stories.answer_zh: 两只耳朵听到声音的时间和强弱有小差别，大脑用它判断方向。左右声道能制造空间感。
stories.answer_en: Our two ears receive small timing and loudness differences, which the brain uses for direction. Left and right channels create space.
stories.fun: 闭眼听音乐，有时能听出乐器在左边。

stories.title_zh: 为什么玻璃杯水量不同音高不同？
stories.title_en: Why does water level change a glass note?
stories.answer_zh: 敲杯子时玻璃和水一起振动。水越多，整体越难快速振动，声音通常越低。
stories.answer_en: When a glass is tapped, glass and water vibrate together. More water makes fast vibration harder, usually lowering the pitch.
stories.fun: 几只杯子装不同水量，可以做小乐器。

stories.title_zh: 为什么真空里听不到声音？
stories.title_en: Why is there no sound in a vacuum?
stories.answer_zh: 声音需要空气、水或固体来传递振动。真空里几乎没有粒子，一个推不到下一个，声音就没法传播。
stories.answer_en: Sound needs air, water or solids to carry vibration. In a vacuum there are almost no particles to push each other.
stories.fun: 太空电影里的爆炸声，是为了观众效果。

stories.title_zh: 为什么噪音会让人累？
stories.title_en: Why can noise make us tired?
stories.answer_zh: 突然、杂乱或太大的声音会让大脑保持警觉，好像随时有危险。时间久了，注意力和情绪都会被消耗。
stories.answer_en: Sudden, messy or loud sound keeps the brain alert as if danger might appear. Over time it drains attention and mood.
stories.fun: 安静不是没有声音，而是声音不打扰你。

stories.title_zh: 为什么羽绒服保暖？
stories.title_en: Why is a down jacket warm?
stories.answer_zh: 羽绒很蓬松，能困住很多不流动的空气。空气传热慢，所以像一层看不见的被子，把身体热量留住。
stories.answer_en: Down traps lots of still air. Still air conducts heat poorly, acting like an invisible blanket around the body.
stories.fun: 被子、泡沫箱、双层窗也常靠困住空气保温。

stories.title_zh: 为什么冰箱后面是热的？
stories.title_en: Why is the back of a fridge warm?
stories.answer_zh: 冰箱不是制造冷，而是把箱内热量搬到外面。后面的管子把热散进房间，所以摸起来热。
stories.answer_en: A fridge does not create cold; it moves heat from inside to outside. The coils at the back release that heat into the room.
stories.fun: 开着冰箱门不能让房间变凉，只会让房间更热。

stories.title_zh: 为什么吹热汤会凉得快？
stories.title_en: Why does blowing cool hot soup?
stories.answer_zh: 吹气会带走汤面上方的热空气和水蒸气，让蒸发变快。蒸发需要带走热量，所以汤凉得更快。
stories.answer_en: Blowing removes warm air and vapour above the soup and speeds evaporation. Evaporation takes heat away, so the soup cools faster.
stories.fun: 摊开在大碗里的汤，也比深杯里的汤凉得快。

stories.title_zh: 为什么夏天地面会烫脚？
stories.title_en: Why is pavement hot in summer?
stories.answer_zh: 深色地面会吸收很多太阳光，并把光能变成热。石头和柏油还很会储热，所以晒久后比空气热得多。
stories.answer_en: Dark pavement absorbs sunlight and turns it into heat. Stone and asphalt store heat well, so they can become much hotter than the air.
stories.fun: 沙滩烫脚也是同一类原因。

stories.title_zh: 为什么空调会滴水？
stories.title_en: Why does an air conditioner drip water?
stories.answer_zh: 空气里的水蒸气碰到空调里的冷管子，会凝结成水滴。空调降温的同时，也把湿气变成水排出去。
stories.answer_en: Water vapour in air condenses on cold coils inside the air conditioner. The AC cools air and removes moisture as water.
stories.fun: 潮湿天开空调舒服，部分原因是除湿。

stories.title_zh: 为什么热空气会上升？
stories.title_en: Why does hot air rise?
stories.answer_zh: 空气受热会膨胀，同样体积里分子变少，密度变小。周围较冷较重的空气把它挤上去。
stories.answer_en: Heated air expands, so the same volume has fewer molecules and becomes less dense. Cooler heavier air pushes it upward.
stories.fun: 热气球就是靠热空气变轻。

stories.title_zh: 为什么汗蒸发会让人凉快？
stories.title_en: Why does evaporating sweat cool us?
stories.answer_zh: 汗水变成水蒸气需要能量，它会从皮肤带走热量。热量被带走，身体就降温。
stories.answer_en: Sweat needs energy to become vapour, and it takes that energy as heat from skin, cooling the body.
stories.fun: 风扇让汗蒸发更快，所以觉得凉。

stories.title_zh: 为什么暖水袋会慢慢变凉？
stories.title_en: Why does a hot-water bottle cool down?
stories.answer_zh: 热量总会从温度高的地方流向温度低的地方。暖水袋把热传给手、被子和空气，自己就慢慢凉了。
stories.answer_en: Heat flows from warmer places to cooler ones. A hot-water bottle gives heat to hands, blanket and air, so it cools.
stories.fun: 保温套是在减慢热量逃跑。

stories.title_zh: 为什么玻璃窗冬天会结水？
stories.title_en: Why do windows collect water in winter?
stories.answer_zh: 室内暖湿空气碰到冷玻璃会降温，冷空气容纳不了那么多水蒸气，多出来的水就变成小水滴。
stories.answer_en: Warm moist indoor air cools on cold glass. Cooler air cannot hold as much vapour, so extra water becomes droplets.
stories.fun: 这和冷饮杯外面出水是同一件事。

stories.title_zh: 为什么烫伤要用流动冷水冲？
stories.title_en: Why cool a burn with running water?
stories.answer_zh: 烫伤后热量还会继续往皮肤深处传。流动冷水能带走热量，减少继续伤害。
stories.answer_en: After a burn, heat can keep moving deeper into skin. Running cool water carries heat away and reduces further damage.
stories.fun: 不要用冰块硬贴，太冷也会伤皮肤。

stories.title_zh: 为什么球会弹起来？
stories.title_en: Why does a ball bounce?
stories.answer_zh: 球撞地时被压扁一点，储存弹性能。恢复形状时，它把能量还回来，把自己推离地面。
stories.answer_en: A ball squashes when it hits the ground and stores elastic energy. As it returns to shape, it pushes itself upward.
stories.fun: 气打得足的球更容易弹。

stories.title_zh: 为什么推门离合页远一点更省力？
stories.title_en: Why is it easier to push a door far from the hinge?
stories.answer_zh: 门绕合页转动。离合页越远，同样的力产生的转动效果越大，所以门把手总在远离合页的一边。
stories.answer_en: A door rotates around its hinge. The farther from the hinge you push, the greater turning effect your force has.
stories.fun: 长扳手拧螺丝省力，也是力矩在帮忙。

stories.title_zh: 为什么书包带宽一点不勒肩？
stories.title_en: Why do wide backpack straps hurt less?
stories.answer_zh: 同样重量压在更大面积上，单位面积受到的压力就小。宽肩带把力分散开，肩膀就舒服些。
stories.answer_en: The same weight spread over a larger area creates less pressure. Wide straps distribute the force over the shoulders.
stories.fun: 雪鞋也是靠增大面积让人不容易陷进雪里。

stories.title_zh: 为什么安全带能保护人？
stories.title_en: Why do seatbelts protect us?
stories.answer_zh: 车突然停下时，人的身体因为惯性还想往前。安全带用较长时间把身体拉停，并防止人撞到车内。
stories.answer_en: When a car stops suddenly, inertia keeps your body moving. A seatbelt slows you over more time and prevents you from hitting the car interior.
stories.fun: 安全气囊需要和安全带配合，不能替代安全带。

stories.title_zh: 为什么自行车动起来后更稳？
stories.title_en: Why is a moving bicycle more stable?
stories.answer_zh: 车轮转动时有保持方向的趋势，前轮还会自动做小调整。速度太慢时这些帮助变弱，就容易晃。
stories.answer_en: Spinning wheels tend to keep direction, and the front wheel makes tiny corrections. At very low speed, these effects weaken.
stories.fun: 会骑车后，大脑在偷偷做很多调整。

stories.title_zh: 为什么滑冰会很滑？
stories.title_en: Why is skating slippery?
stories.answer_zh: 冰面光滑，冰刀和冰之间摩擦小。压力和摩擦还可能让表面出现极薄水层，像润滑一样。
stories.answer_en: Ice is smooth with low friction. Pressure and rubbing may create a very thin water layer that acts like lubrication.
stories.fun: 太冷的冰反而没那么好滑。

stories.title_zh: 为什么滑梯越陡越快？
stories.title_en: Why is a steeper slide faster?
stories.answer_zh: 重力可以分成沿滑梯向下的部分和压向滑梯的部分。滑梯越陡，向下拉你的那部分越大。
stories.answer_en: Gravity can be split into a part down the slide and a part pressing into it. A steeper slide has a larger downward part.
stories.fun: 摩擦会抢走一部分速度。

stories.title_zh: 为什么陀螺转着不容易倒？
stories.title_en: Why does a spinning top stay upright?
stories.answer_zh: 快速旋转的物体有保持转动方向的本领，叫角动量。摩擦慢慢消耗能量后，陀螺才会倒。
stories.answer_en: A fast-spinning object tends to keep its rotation direction; this is angular momentum. Friction slowly drains energy until it falls.
stories.fun: 地球也像一个巨大的旋转陀螺。

stories.title_zh: 为什么羽毛比石头落得慢？
stories.title_en: Why does a feather fall slower than a stone?
stories.answer_zh: 重力都拉它们向下，但空气阻力对轻薄羽毛影响很大。石头更重更紧实，空气推不住它。
stories.answer_en: Gravity pulls both down, but air resistance affects a light broad feather much more. A dense stone is harder for air to slow.
stories.fun: 在真空里，羽毛和锤子会同时落地。

stories.title_zh: 为什么拔河不只是比力气？
stories.title_en: Why is tug-of-war not only about strength?
stories.answer_zh: 人要靠脚和地面的摩擦把力传出去。姿势、鞋底、节奏和团队配合都会影响结果。
stories.answer_en: Players need friction between feet and ground to transmit force. Posture, shoes, rhythm and teamwork all matter.
stories.fun: 地面很滑时，再有力也使不上劲。

categories.title: 天空与宇宙
categories.subtitle: 从窗外天气到夜空星星

stories.title_zh: 为什么天空是蓝色的？
stories.title_en: Why is the sky blue?
stories.answer_zh: 阳光进入大气后，蓝色光比红色光更容易被空气分子散射到四面八方。我们从各个方向看到很多蓝光，所以天空显蓝。
stories.answer_en: When sunlight enters the atmosphere, blue light is scattered by air molecules more than red light. Blue reaches our eyes from all directions.
stories.fun: 傍晚阳光走过更厚的大气，蓝光散掉，红橙光留下。

stories.title_zh: 为什么云有白有黑？
stories.title_en: Why are some clouds white and some dark?
stories.answer_zh: 薄云能把阳光散射出来，看起来白。厚云挡住很多光，云底光线少，就显得灰黑，像一把大伞。
stories.answer_en: Thin clouds scatter sunlight and look white. Thick clouds block much of the light, so their bases look grey or dark.
stories.fun: 黑云不脏，它只是太厚，光穿不过来。

stories.title_zh: 为什么会下雨？
stories.title_en: Why does it rain?
stories.answer_zh: 水蒸气升到高处变冷，凝结成小水滴。水滴在云里不断碰撞变大，空气托不住时，就落下来成雨。
stories.answer_en: Water vapour rises, cools and condenses into droplets. Droplets collide and grow until air can no longer hold them, then they fall as rain.
stories.fun: 雨滴不是泪滴形，更像被空气压扁的小圆包。

stories.title_zh: 为什么先看见闪电，后听见雷？
stories.title_en: Why do we see lightning before hearing thunder?
stories.answer_zh: 闪电和雷几乎同时产生，但光跑得极快，声音在空气中慢很多。所以远处打雷时，光先到，声音后到。
stories.answer_en: Lightning and thunder happen almost together, but light travels far faster than sound, so the flash reaches us first.
stories.fun: 数闪电后几秒听到雷，可以粗略估计雷雨离你多远。

stories.title_zh: 为什么会有风？
stories.title_en: Why does wind blow?
stories.answer_zh: 太阳把地面加热得不均匀，有些地方空气热而轻会上升，旁边冷空气补过来，空气流动就成了风。
stories.answer_en: The Sun heats Earth unevenly. Warm light air rises, and cooler air moves in to replace it; moving air is wind.
stories.fun: 风其实是空气在搬家。

stories.title_zh: 为什么台风会旋转？
stories.title_en: Why do typhoons spin?
stories.answer_zh: 温暖海水让空气上升形成低压，四周空气流进来。地球自转会让空气偏转，巨大云团就旋转起来。
stories.answer_en: Warm ocean water makes air rise and creates low pressure. Incoming air is bent by Earth’s rotation, making the storm spin.
stories.fun: 台风像靠海水热量供能的大旋涡。

stories.title_zh: 为什么雾天看不远？
stories.title_en: Why is it hard to see far in fog?
stories.answer_zh: 雾里有无数小水滴，它们会把光散射到很多方向。远处物体的光被打乱，眼睛就看不清。
stories.answer_en: Fog contains countless tiny droplets that scatter light in many directions, scrambling light from distant objects.
stories.fun: 雾不是烟，是贴近地面的云。

stories.title_zh: 为什么雪花有六个角？
stories.title_en: Why do snowflakes have six sides?
stories.answer_zh: 水分子结成冰晶时，常按六角形方式排列。雪花长大时沿着这个结构扩展，所以常见六角对称。
stories.answer_en: Water molecules in ice often arrange in hexagonal patterns. Snowflakes grow from that structure, producing sixfold symmetry.
stories.fun: 每片雪花经历的温度和湿度不同，所以形状也不同。

stories.title_zh: 为什么冰雹会从天上掉下来？
stories.title_en: Why does hail fall from the sky?
stories.answer_zh: 强烈上升气流把小冰粒在云里反复托上去，水滴一层层冻在外面。冰粒太重后，就掉下来成冰雹。
stories.answer_en: Strong updrafts lift ice pellets again and again inside a storm cloud. Water freezes in layers until the stones become too heavy and fall.
stories.fun: 切开大冰雹，有时能看到像洋葱一样的层。

stories.title_zh: 为什么冬天呼气会有白气？
stories.title_en: Why can we see our breath in winter?
stories.answer_zh: 呼出的气里有水蒸气，遇到冷空气后凝结成许多小水滴。我们看到的白气就是这些小水滴。
stories.answer_en: Your breath contains water vapour. Cold air makes it condense into tiny droplets, which appear as white mist.
stories.fun: 这和热水白雾是同一个道理。

stories.title_zh: 为什么月亮会变圆变缺？
stories.title_en: Why does the Moon change shape?
stories.answer_zh: 月亮本身不发光，只反射太阳光。它绕地球转时，我们看到被太阳照亮的部分角度不同，于是有月牙、半月、满月。
stories.answer_en: The Moon reflects sunlight. As it orbits Earth, we see different parts of its lit half, creating crescents, half moons and full moons.
stories.fun: 月亮没有真的被吃掉，只是亮的那面换了角度。

stories.title_zh: 为什么星星会眨眼？
stories.title_en: Why do stars twinkle?
stories.answer_zh: 星光穿过不断流动的冷热空气时，会被轻轻折弯。光一会强一会弱，看起来就像星星在眨眼。
stories.answer_en: Starlight is bent by moving layers of warm and cool air, making it flicker as if the star is twinkling.
stories.fun: 行星通常不太闪，因为它们在天空中不是一个极小点。

stories.title_zh: 为什么太阳看起来每天东升西落？
stories.title_en: Why does the Sun seem to rise and set?
stories.answer_zh: 其实主要是地球自己在转。我们站在转动的地球上，就像坐旋转木马，看见太阳从一边出现、另一边离开。
stories.answer_en: Earth is rotating. Standing on a spinning planet makes the Sun appear to rise on one side and set on the other.
stories.fun: 太阳没有每天绕着我们跑一圈，是地球在转。

stories.title_zh: 为什么白天看不见星星？
stories.title_en: Why can’t we see stars in daytime?
stories.answer_zh: 星星白天还在那里，只是太阳光被大气散射后让天空太亮。弱弱的星光被明亮天空盖住了。
stories.answer_en: Stars are still there in daytime, but scattered sunlight makes the sky so bright that faint starlight is hidden.
stories.fun: 月亮白天有时能看见，因为它比星星亮得多。

stories.title_zh: 为什么太阳会发光发热？
stories.title_en: Why does the Sun shine and heat us?
stories.answer_zh: 太阳中心极热极压，氢原子会结合成氦，释放巨大能量。这种能量一路传到表面，变成光和热来到地球。
stories.answer_en: In the Sun’s hot dense core, hydrogen atoms fuse into helium and release huge energy. That energy travels outward as light and heat.
stories.fun: 太阳像一个天然核聚变炉。

stories.title_zh: 为什么月亮看起来跟太阳差不多大？
stories.title_en: Why do the Sun and Moon look similar in size?
stories.answer_zh: 太阳其实比月亮大得多，但也远得多。远近和大小刚好配合，让它们在天空中看起来差不多大。
stories.answer_en: The Sun is much larger than the Moon, but also much farther away. Size and distance combine so they look similar in the sky.
stories.fun: 这也是日食能那么精彩的原因。

stories.title_zh: 为什么会发生日食？
stories.title_en: Why does a solar eclipse happen?
stories.answer_zh: 日食时，月亮正好走到太阳和地球之间，挡住一部分或全部太阳光。月亮的影子落到地球上，人就看到日食。
stories.answer_en: A solar eclipse happens when the Moon moves between Sun and Earth, blocking some or all sunlight and casting a shadow on Earth.
stories.fun: 看日食必须用专门保护眼睛的方法。

stories.title_zh: 为什么会发生月食？
stories.title_en: Why does a lunar eclipse happen?
stories.answer_zh: 月食时，地球走到太阳和月亮之间，地球影子盖住月亮。月亮可能变暗，甚至呈红铜色。
stories.answer_en: A lunar eclipse happens when Earth moves between Sun and Moon, and Earth’s shadow covers the Moon. The Moon can turn dim or coppery red.
stories.fun: 红色来自地球大气把阳光弯过去。

stories.title_zh: 为什么北斗七星位置会变？
stories.title_en: Why do star patterns move across the sky?
stories.answer_zh: 一夜之间星星位置变化，主要是地球自转造成的；一年中同一时间看到的星空变化，则和地球绕太阳公转有关。
stories.answer_en: Stars appear to move during a night because Earth rotates. Seasonal changes in the night sky come from Earth orbiting the Sun.
stories.fun: 星空像一本随时间翻页的书。

stories.title_zh: 为什么流星会发亮？
stories.title_en: Why does a meteor glow?
stories.answer_zh: 小石块高速冲进大气，压缩和摩擦周围空气，让它和自己表面剧烈发热发光。
stories.answer_en: A small space rock enters the atmosphere at high speed, heating the air and its surface until it glows.
stories.fun: 流星不是星星掉下来，而是小碎片烧亮了。

stories.title_zh: 为什么行星不会掉进太阳？
stories.title_en: Why don’t planets fall into the Sun?
stories.answer_zh: 太阳的引力一直拉着行星，但行星也有向前飞的速度。它们一边被拉向太阳，一边向前错过太阳，就形成轨道。
stories.answer_en: The Sun’s gravity pulls planets inward, but planets also move forward. They keep falling toward the Sun while missing it, creating orbits.
stories.fun: 绕地球飞的卫星也是一直“掉着但错过”。

stories.title_zh: 为什么火星是红色的？
stories.title_en: Why is Mars red?
stories.answer_zh: 火星表面有很多含铁矿物，和氧气反应后像生锈一样变成红褐色。大量红色尘土让火星看起来发红。
stories.answer_en: Mars has iron-rich minerals that reacted with oxygen, a bit like rust. Red dust on the surface makes the planet look reddish.
stories.fun: 火星像一个被铁锈粉末盖住的世界。

stories.title_zh: 为什么土星有光环？
stories.title_en: Why does Saturn have rings?
stories.answer_zh: 土星光环由无数冰块和岩石碎片组成，有的像沙粒，有的像房子。它们一起绕土星转，远看像一圈平滑的环。
stories.answer_en: Saturn’s rings are made of countless pieces of ice and rock, from grains to house-sized chunks, all orbiting Saturn.
stories.fun: 光环不是一整片硬盘，而是一大群碎片。

stories.title_zh: 为什么地球适合生命？
stories.title_en: Why is Earth good for life?
stories.answer_zh: 地球离太阳不太近也不太远，有液态水、合适大气和稳定温度范围。生命需要的许多条件在这里刚好凑齐。
stories.answer_en: Earth is neither too close nor too far from the Sun, has liquid water, a useful atmosphere and a stable temperature range.
stories.fun: 这不代表只有地球有生命，但地球确实很幸运。

stories.title_zh: 为什么金星那么亮？
stories.title_en: Why is Venus so bright?
stories.answer_zh: 金星离地球比较近，表面又被厚厚云层包围，云层很会反射阳光，所以它在夜空中非常亮。
stories.answer_en: Venus is fairly close to Earth and covered in thick clouds that reflect sunlight strongly, making it very bright.
stories.fun: 它常被叫作晨星或昏星。

stories.title_zh: 为什么木星有大红斑？
stories.title_en: Why does Jupiter have the Great Red Spot?
stories.answer_zh: 大红斑是木星上的巨大风暴，已经持续了很久。木星没有像地球这样的坚硬地面，风暴不容易被山脉和陆地打散。
stories.answer_en: The Great Red Spot is a huge storm on Jupiter that has lasted a long time. Jupiter has no solid ground like Earth to break storms apart.
stories.fun: 这个风暴大到能装下地球。

stories.title_zh: 为什么冥王星不再算大行星？
stories.title_en: Why is Pluto not counted as a major planet now?
stories.answer_zh: 科学家给行星定了更严格标准：它要绕太阳转、接近圆形，还要清理自己轨道附近的许多小天体。冥王星没做到最后一点。
stories.answer_en: Scientists set stricter planet rules: orbit the Sun, be nearly round, and clear many small objects near its orbit. Pluto does not meet the last one.
stories.fun: 冥王星没有消失，只是分类变了。

stories.title_zh: 为什么小行星有时会撞地球？
stories.title_en: Why can asteroids hit Earth?
stories.answer_zh: 小行星绕太阳运行，轨道有时会和地球轨道相交。如果两个天体刚好同时到达交叉处，就可能相撞。
stories.answer_en: Asteroids orbit the Sun, and some paths cross Earth’s orbit. If both arrive at the crossing at the same time, a collision can happen.
stories.fun: 科学家一直在监测近地小行星。

stories.title_zh: 为什么彗星有尾巴？
stories.title_en: Why do comets have tails?
stories.answer_zh: 彗星靠近太阳时，冰受热变成气体，带着尘埃喷出来。太阳风和阳光把气体尘埃推成尾巴。
stories.answer_en: When a comet nears the Sun, ice turns to gas and carries dust away. Sunlight and solar wind push the gas and dust into tails.
stories.fun: 彗星尾巴通常背向太阳，不一定跟在后面。

stories.title_zh: 为什么地球有磁场？
stories.title_en: Why does Earth have a magnetic field?
stories.answer_zh: 地球内部有流动的液态金属，运动会产生磁场。磁场能挡住一部分来自太阳的带电粒子。
stories.answer_en: Flowing liquid metal inside Earth helps generate a magnetic field, which deflects some charged particles from the Sun.
stories.fun: 指南针能指北，就是因为地球像个大磁铁。

stories.title_zh: 为什么火箭要带那么多燃料？
stories.title_en: Why do rockets need so much fuel?
stories.answer_zh: 火箭要摆脱地球引力，还要把自己和货物加速到很高速度。燃料不仅要推货物，也要先推剩下的燃料，所以需要很多。
stories.answer_en: A rocket must fight gravity and reach huge speed. It must lift the payload and also lift the remaining fuel, so it needs a lot.
stories.fun: 火箭起飞时大部分重量其实是燃料。

stories.title_zh: 为什么火箭要分级？
stories.title_en: Why do rockets have stages?
stories.answer_zh: 用完燃料的空壳很重，继续带着会浪费力气。火箭把用完的部分丢掉，剩下部分变轻，就能飞得更快更远。
stories.answer_en: Empty fuel tanks are heavy. Rockets drop used stages so the remaining rocket becomes lighter and can fly faster and farther.
stories.fun: 像爬山时把空水瓶扔掉，但火箭版更壮观。

stories.title_zh: 为什么卫星能给手机导航？
stories.title_en: How can satellites help phone navigation?
stories.answer_zh: 卫星会不断发出自己位置和时间信号。手机接收多颗卫星的信号，比较到达时间，就能算出自己在哪里。
stories.answer_en: Satellites send their position and time. A phone receives signals from several satellites and compares arrival times to calculate its location.
stories.fun: GPS不是卫星看见你，而是手机听见卫星。

stories.title_zh: 为什么宇航员会飘起来？
stories.title_en: Why do astronauts float?
stories.answer_zh: 空间站和宇航员都在绕地球自由下落，只是速度快到一直错过地面。他们一起下落，所以彼此感觉像没有重量。
stories.answer_en: The space station and astronauts are falling around Earth together, moving so fast they keep missing the ground. Falling together creates weightlessness.
stories.fun: 失重不是没有重力，而是大家一起掉。

stories.title_zh: 为什么宇航服那么厚？
stories.title_en: Why are spacesuits so bulky?
stories.answer_zh: 太空没有可呼吸空气，温度变化极端，还有微小碎片和辐射。宇航服要提供压力、氧气、保温和保护，所以很复杂。
stories.answer_en: Space has no breathable air, extreme temperatures, tiny debris and radiation. A suit must provide pressure, oxygen, insulation and protection.
stories.fun: 宇航服更像一艘穿在身上的小飞船。

stories.title_zh: 为什么空间站绕地球不会停下来？
stories.title_en: Why doesn’t the space station stop orbiting?
stories.answer_zh: 太空中空气很稀薄，阻力小，空间站能保持很高速度。不过仍有一点阻力，所以偶尔需要推进器提升轨道。
stories.answer_en: Air is extremely thin in orbit, so drag is small and the station keeps high speed. Tiny drag remains, so boosts are sometimes needed.
stories.fun: 轨道不是永动机，但很接近“少阻力滑行”。

stories.title_zh: 为什么返回舱回地球会发热？
stories.title_en: Why does a re-entry capsule get hot?
stories.answer_zh: 返回舱高速冲进大气，会强烈压缩前方空气，使空气和外壳表面升温。隔热层要保护里面的人和设备。
stories.answer_en: A capsule enters the atmosphere at high speed, strongly compressing air in front and heating the air and surface. Heat shields protect inside.
stories.fun: 它不是简单摩擦生热，压缩空气也很重要。

stories.title_zh: 为什么卫星要用太阳能板？
stories.title_en: Why do satellites use solar panels?
stories.answer_zh: 卫星在太空中很难换电池，但阳光很充足。太阳能板把光能变成电能，给仪器持续供电。
stories.answer_en: Satellites cannot easily change batteries, but sunlight is abundant. Solar panels turn light into electricity for instruments.
stories.fun: 卫星进地球影子时，会暂时靠电池工作。

stories.title_zh: 为什么火箭发射那么响？
stories.title_en: Why are rocket launches so loud?
stories.answer_zh: 火箭发动机每秒喷出大量高速热气，猛烈推动空气，产生巨大的声波和震动。
stories.answer_en: Rocket engines throw out huge amounts of hot gas at high speed, violently pushing air and making powerful sound waves.
stories.fun: 发射台会喷水，一部分原因是吸收声音和保护结构。

stories.title_zh: 为什么太空探测器能飞那么远？
stories.title_en: How can space probes travel so far?
stories.answer_zh: 探测器在太空中阻力很小，一旦获得速度就能长期滑行。它们还会借行星引力“甩一把”，改变速度和方向。
stories.answer_en: Space probes face little resistance, so once moving they coast for years. They can also use planet gravity assists to change speed and direction.
stories.fun: 这像借路过的大行星帮忙推一下。

stories.title_zh: 为什么会有四季？
stories.title_en: Why do seasons happen?
stories.answer_zh: 地球自转轴是倾斜的。某个半球更朝向太阳时，白天更长、阳光更直，就是夏天；反过来就是冬天。
stories.answer_en: Earth’s axis is tilted. When one hemisphere tilts toward the Sun, it gets longer days and more direct sunlight, making summer.
stories.fun: 四季不是因为地球离太阳忽远忽近。

stories.title_zh: 为什么夏天白天更长？
stories.title_en: Why are days longer in summer?
stories.answer_zh: 夏天我们所在的半球朝向太阳，地球转一圈时，这一边停留在阳光中的时间更长，所以白天变长。
stories.answer_en: In summer, our hemisphere tilts toward the Sun, so during one rotation it spends more time in sunlight.
stories.fun: 越靠近两极，夏冬白天长度差别越大。

stories.title_zh: 为什么山上更冷？
stories.title_en: Why is it colder on mountains?
stories.answer_zh: 海拔越高，空气越稀薄，能保存的热量少。空气上升时还会膨胀降温，所以山顶通常比山脚冷。
stories.answer_en: Higher altitude has thinner air that holds less heat. Rising air expands and cools, so mountaintops are usually colder.
stories.fun: 有些高山即使在热带也有积雪。

stories.title_zh: 为什么城市比郊外热？
stories.title_en: Why are cities hotter than the countryside?
stories.answer_zh: 城市有很多柏油、水泥和楼房，会吸收并储存太阳热量；车和空调也排热。树少时降温更慢。
stories.answer_en: Asphalt, concrete and buildings absorb and store heat, while cars and air conditioners add more. Fewer trees mean slower cooling.
stories.fun: 这叫城市热岛效应。

stories.title_zh: 为什么同一个城市每天日落时间会变？
stories.title_en: Why does sunset time change through the year?
stories.answer_zh: 地球轴倾斜加上绕太阳公转，让太阳在天空中的路径一年中不断改变。路径长短变了，日落时间也变。
stories.answer_en: Earth’s tilted axis and orbit make the Sun’s path in the sky change through the year, changing sunset time.
stories.fun: 冬天日落早，不是太阳累了，是路径变了。

stories.title_zh: 为什么靠海的地方冬暖夏凉？
stories.title_en: Why are coastal places milder?
stories.answer_zh: 水升温和降温都比陆地慢。海洋像一个巨大的温度缓冲垫，夏天吸热，冬天放热。
stories.answer_en: Water warms and cools more slowly than land. The ocean acts like a huge temperature buffer, absorbing heat in summer and releasing it in winter.
stories.fun: 这也是海边昼夜温差常较小的原因。
