stories.answer_zh: 电梯有钢缆、制动器、限速器和多套安全装置。即使某个部分出问题，其他系统也会帮忙刹住。
stories.answer_en: Elevators have cables, brakes, speed governors and multiple safety systems, so other systems can stop the car if one part fails.
stories.fun: 现代电梯比很多人想象中安全。

stories.title_zh: 为什么机器人能避开障碍？
stories.title_en: How can robots avoid obstacles?
stories.answer_zh: 机器人用摄像头、超声波或激光雷达感知周围。电脑把信号变成地图，再计算安全路线。
stories.answer_en: Robots use cameras, ultrasound or lidar to sense surroundings. A computer turns signals into a map and plans a safe path.
stories.fun: 扫地机器人每天都在做小型导航。

stories.title_zh: 为什么洗衣机会甩干衣服？
stories.title_en: Why can a washing machine spin clothes dry?
stories.answer_zh: 洗衣机高速旋转时，衣服和水都想沿着原来的方向继续运动。水比较容易从衣服缝隙和桶壁小孔里被甩出去，衣服留在桶里，于是水分减少了。它不是把水烤干，而是用快速旋转把水分离出去。
stories.answer_en: When a washing machine spins fast, clothes and water tend to keep moving outward. Water can escape through gaps in the clothes and holes in the drum, while the clothes stay inside. The machine is not heating the water away; it is separating water by spinning.
stories.fun: 衣服挤在一边时洗衣机乱晃，是因为重量没有分布均匀。

stories.title_zh: 为什么冰箱门上有磁条？
stories.title_en: Why does a fridge door have a magnetic strip?
stories.answer_zh: 冰箱门边的磁条能把门轻轻吸紧，让冷空气不容易跑出来，热空气不容易钻进去。如果密封不好，冰箱就要更努力工作，里面还可能结霜或温度不稳。磁条像冰箱门的温柔拉链，把冷气关在家里。
stories.answer_en: The magnetic strip around a fridge door helps seal it tightly. Cold air stays in, and warm air stays out. If the seal is poor, the fridge must work harder, and frost or uneven temperature can appear. The strip is like a gentle zipper for cold air.
stories.fun: 夹一张纸在冰箱门缝里，如果很容易拉出来，密封可能变差了。

stories.title_zh: 为什么风扇让人凉快，却没有把房间变冷？
stories.title_en: Why does a fan cool you but not the room?
stories.answer_zh: 风扇主要是让空气流动。流动的空气能更快带走皮肤上的热，也能帮助汗水蒸发，而蒸发会带走热量，所以你觉得凉。可是风扇电机本身还会产生一点热，所以关着门空转的风扇不会真正把房间变冷。
stories.answer_en: A fan mainly moves air. Moving air carries heat away from your skin faster and helps sweat evaporate, which removes heat, so you feel cooler. But the fan motor also produces a little heat, so a fan running in a closed empty room does not truly cool the room.
stories.fun: 人不在房间时开风扇，通常是在给空气表演。

stories.title_zh: 为什么吸尘器能吸走灰尘？
stories.title_en: Why can a vacuum cleaner suck up dust?
stories.answer_zh: 吸尘器里面的风扇快速转动，把机器内部的一部分空气推走，形成较低的气压。外面的空气就带着灰尘从吸头冲进去，想把压力补平。灰尘不是被神秘力量吸走，而是搭上了一股很急的空气列车。
stories.answer_en: A vacuum cleaner has a fast fan that moves air out of part of the machine, creating lower pressure inside. Outside air rushes in through the nozzle, carrying dust with it. The dust is not pulled by magic; it rides a fast train of moving air.
stories.fun: 滤网堵住后，空气列车跑不动，吸力就会变弱。

stories.title_zh: 为什么电饭锅会自动跳到保温？
stories.title_en: Why does a rice cooker switch to keep-warm automatically?
stories.answer_zh: 煮饭时，只要锅里还有很多水，温度通常会停在水沸腾附近。等米吸水、锅里自由的水变少后，锅底温度会开始升高。电饭锅的传感器发现温度变化，就知道饭差不多好了，于是跳到保温。它是在听温度讲故事。
stories.answer_en: While rice cooks and plenty of water remains, the temperature stays near boiling. When the rice absorbs water and less free water is left, the bottom temperature rises. A sensor notices this change and switches the cooker to keep-warm. It is reading the story told by temperature.
stories.fun: 不同米和水量会影响口感，因为吸水和加热过程不完全一样。

stories.title_zh: 为什么打印机会一行一行地打印？
stories.title_en: Why do printers print line by line?
stories.answer_zh: 打印机要把电脑里的图片或文字变成纸上的许多小点。喷墨打印机会控制小喷嘴喷出 tiny 墨滴；激光打印机则用光和静电安排碳粉的位置。它们通常按行或按区域逐步完成，就像用非常快的手把一幅图织出来。
stories.answer_en: A printer turns digital text or images into many tiny dots on paper. Inkjet printers control tiny nozzles that spray ink droplets. Laser printers use light and static electricity to place toner. They build the page line by line or area by area, like weaving an image very quickly.
stories.fun: 照片打印清楚，是因为小点足够密，眼睛把它们看成连续画面。

stories.title_zh: 电脑为什么会发热？
stories.title_en: Why do computers get hot?
stories.answer_zh: 芯片里有大量微小开关高速开合，电流流动时会产生热。风扇和散热片负责把热带走。
stories.answer_en: Chips contain huge numbers of tiny switches working fast. Electric current creates heat, and fans and heat sinks carry it away.
stories.fun: 风扇大声转，可能是电脑在努力降温。

stories.title_zh: 为什么电脑只懂0和1？
stories.title_en: Why do computers use zeros and ones?
stories.answer_zh: 电脑芯片里的开关最容易稳定地区分“开”和“关”两种状态。用0和1表示这两种状态，就能组合出文字、图片和声音。
stories.answer_en: Computer switches reliably distinguish two states: off and on. Zeros and ones represent those states and can build text, images and sound.
stories.fun: 简单的0和1，组合起来能很复杂。

stories.title_zh: AI为什么会回答问题？
stories.title_en: How can AI answer questions?
stories.answer_zh: AI读过大量文字，从中学会词语和句子之间的规律。回答时，它根据问题和上下文预测接下来最合适的内容。
stories.answer_en: AI studies huge amounts of text and learns patterns between words and ideas. It answers by predicting suitable content from the question and context.
stories.fun: AI会犯错，所以重要内容要学会核对。

stories.title_zh: 为什么密码不能太简单？
stories.title_en: Why should passwords not be too simple?
stories.answer_zh: 电脑能很快尝试常见密码。长而独特的密码组合多得多，别人猜中的难度就大很多。
stories.answer_en: Computers can try common passwords quickly. Long unique passwords have far more combinations and are much harder to guess.
stories.fun: 生日和123456像把钥匙放在门口。

stories.title_zh: 为什么电脑需要内存？
stories.title_en: Why does a computer need memory?
stories.answer_zh: 硬盘像仓库，能长期保存东西；内存像书桌，电脑正在用的资料会先放在这里。书桌越大，同时摊开的书和作业越多，找起来越快。关机后，普通内存里的东西会清空，所以重要文件要存进硬盘或云端。
stories.answer_en: A drive is like a storage room for long-term keeping. Memory is like a desk where the computer places information it is using right now. A bigger desk can hold more open books and papers, making work faster. When power is off, ordinary memory clears, so files must be saved elsewhere.
stories.fun: 电脑开太多程序变慢，常常就是“书桌”太挤了。

stories.title_zh: 为什么删除的文件有时还能恢复？
stories.title_en: Why can deleted files sometimes be recovered?
stories.answer_zh: 删除文件时，电脑常常先把目录里的“这里有文件”标记擦掉，但文件内容可能还留在硬盘某处，直到新的数据覆盖它。就像图书馆把索引卡拿走了，书可能还在架子上。恢复软件就是去找那些还没被新内容盖住的书页。
stories.answer_en: When a file is deleted, the computer often removes the “file is here” label from its directory, but the actual data may remain until new data overwrites it. It is like removing an index card while the book may still be on the shelf. Recovery tools search for pages not yet covered.
stories.fun: 真正安全删除会更麻烦，因为要把原来的内容覆盖掉。

stories.title_zh: 为什么软件总是要更新？
stories.title_en: Why do apps and software keep updating?
stories.answer_zh: 软件像一座一直有人使用的房子。住久了会发现门锁要加固、窗户漏风、房间布局可以更好。更新可能修复错误、堵住安全漏洞、适配新设备，也可能增加功能。不是每个更新都让人兴奋，但很多更新是在让房子更稳。
stories.answer_en: Software is like a house people keep living in. Over time, you discover locks that need strengthening, windows that leak, or rooms that could work better. Updates can fix bugs, close security holes, support new devices, or add features. Many updates make the house sturdier.
stories.fun: 重要安全更新最好别拖太久，因为漏洞可能被坏人利用。

stories.title_zh: 为什么 AI 有时会一本正经地说错？
stories.title_en: Why does AI sometimes sound confident but wrong?
stories.answer_zh: 很多 AI 的本领是根据大量文字经验预测“接下来像是什么”。它很会组织语言，但不等于每句话都亲眼检查过事实。如果问题很细、资料混乱，AI 可能把看起来合理的东西拼出来，却不是真的。用 AI 时要像用聪明草稿本：有帮助，但重要事实要核对。
stories.answer_en: Many AI systems are trained to predict what text is likely to come next based on huge amounts of writing. They are good at language, but that does not mean every fact has been checked. For detailed or messy topics, AI can produce something plausible but false. Treat it like a smart draft partner and verify important facts.
stories.fun: 会提好问题、会查证答案，是未来很重要的学习能力。

stories.title_zh: 为什么芯片做得越小越厉害？
stories.title_en: Why can smaller chips be more powerful?
stories.answer_zh: 芯片里有大量微小开关。开关越小，同样面积里能放下的开关越多，信号走的路也更短，计算就可能更快、更省电。但太小也带来散热和制造难题。芯片不是简单变小就赢，而是在极小的世界里保持秩序。
stories.answer_en: Chips contain huge numbers of tiny switches. Smaller switches allow more to fit in the same area, and signals travel shorter distances, which can make computing faster and more efficient. But smaller also creates heat and manufacturing challenges. The trick is keeping order in an extremely tiny world.
stories.fun: 现代芯片里的结构小到用普通显微镜都看不清。

stories.title_zh: 为什么电脑中毒叫“病毒”？
stories.title_en: Why is malicious software called a virus?
stories.answer_zh: 电脑病毒和生物病毒不一样，但它们有一个相似点：会复制和传播。恶意程序可能偷偷把自己放进别的文件或电脑里，造成破坏、偷信息或拖慢系统。人们借用了“病毒”这个词，是因为它像数字世界里会传染的坏代码。
stories.answer_en: A computer virus is not a biological virus, but it has a similar idea: copying and spreading. Malicious software may secretly place itself in other files or computers, causing damage, stealing information, or slowing systems. The word “virus” is used because it behaves like infectious bad code.
stories.fun: 不乱点陌生链接，就像不给陌生坏代码开门。

stories.title_zh: 为什么飞机那么重还能飞？
stories.title_en: Why can heavy airplanes fly?
stories.answer_zh: 机翼让空气流动产生压力差，同时把空气向下推。空气反过来给飞机向上的升力，只要升力够大就能飞。
stories.answer_en: Wings create pressure differences and push air downward. Air pushes back upward as lift; if lift is large enough, the plane flies.
stories.fun: 飞机不是靠轻，而是靠足够升力。

stories.title_zh: 为什么飞机起飞前要加速？
stories.title_en: Why must planes speed up before takeoff?
stories.answer_zh: 机翼需要足够快的空气流过，才能产生足够升力。飞机在跑道上加速，就是在给机翼制造强气流。
stories.answer_en: Wings need fast airflow to create enough lift. A plane accelerates on the runway to make strong airflow over the wings.
stories.fun: 逆风起飞反而有帮助，因为相对气流更强。

stories.title_zh: 导航为什么知道我们在哪里？
stories.title_en: Why does navigation know where we are?
stories.answer_zh: 手机接收多颗GPS卫星发来的时间信号，比较信号到达时间差，就能算出自己位置。
stories.answer_en: A phone receives timing signals from several GPS satellites. Comparing arrival times lets it calculate its position.
stories.fun: GPS不是卫星看见你，是手机听见卫星。

stories.title_zh: 为什么高铁车头是尖的？
stories.title_en: Why are high-speed train noses pointed?
stories.answer_zh: 高速行驶时空气阻力很大。尖长车头能更平顺地把空气分开，减少阻力和噪音。
stories.answer_en: At high speed, air resistance is large. A long pointed nose parts the air smoothly, reducing drag and noise.
stories.fun: 工程师还要考虑进隧道时的压力波。

stories.title_zh: 为什么汽车安全气囊能瞬间鼓起来？
stories.title_en: Why can a car airbag inflate instantly?
stories.answer_zh: 汽车里有传感器能判断突然减速是不是严重碰撞。一旦需要保护乘客，系统会在极短时间内产生大量气体，把气囊撑开。气囊不是软枕头那么简单，它的任务是在身体撞到硬东西之前，先用可控的方式接住一部分冲击。
stories.answer_en: Cars have sensors that detect sudden deceleration and decide whether a serious crash is happening. If protection is needed, the system quickly produces a large amount of gas to inflate the airbag. It is not just a soft pillow; it catches part of the impact before the body hits something hard.
stories.fun: 安全气囊要配合安全带才更有效，单独使用可能反而危险。

stories.title_zh: 为什么地铁进站时会有一阵风？
stories.title_en: Why is there a gust of wind when a subway train arrives?
stories.answer_zh: 地铁列车在隧道里前进时，会把前面的空气推着走。隧道空间比较窄，空气被挤到站台附近，就变成一阵明显的风。列车像一个巨大的活塞，在地下管道里推空气。
stories.answer_en: As a subway train moves through a tunnel, it pushes air ahead of it. The tunnel is narrow, so the air is squeezed toward the platform and becomes a noticeable gust. The train acts like a huge piston pushing air through an underground tube.
stories.fun: 站在安全线后面，不只是为了离车远，也能避开突然气流和人群晃动。

stories.title_zh: 为什么大船那么重还能浮起来？
stories.title_en: Why can a huge heavy ship float?
stories.answer_zh: 船能浮起来，不是因为它轻，而是因为它排开的水足够重。船体做成空心的大形状，整体平均密度比水小，水就能给它足够的向上托力。把一块钢丢进水里会沉，但把钢做成大船，它就能带着空气一起浮。
stories.answer_en: A ship floats not because it is light, but because it pushes aside enough water. Its hollow shape makes its average density lower than water, so the water provides enough upward buoyant force. A block of steel sinks, but steel shaped into a large ship can float with air inside.
stories.fun: 船进水后会变危险，因为空气空间减少，平均密度变大。

stories.title_zh: 为什么轮船需要舵？
stories.title_en: Why does a ship need a rudder?
stories.answer_zh: 船在水里前进时，水会从船尾流过。舵像一块可以转动的板，改变水流方向，水流反过来推船尾，船头就会慢慢转向。船不是像汽车那样用轮子抓地转弯，而是用水流来帮它转身。
stories.answer_en: As a ship moves, water flows past its stern. A rudder is a movable plate that changes the direction of that flow. The water pushes back on the stern, and the bow slowly turns. A ship does not turn by gripping a road like a car; it turns by using flowing water.
stories.fun: 船越大，转弯通常越慢，所以船长要很早就计划方向。

stories.title_zh: 为什么飞机窗户是圆角的？
stories.title_en: Why do airplane windows have rounded corners?
stories.answer_zh: 飞机飞高后，机舱内外压力不同，窗户周围会承受很大的力。尖角容易让力量集中，像纸从小缺口处更容易撕开。圆角能让力分散得更均匀，减少裂缝从角落开始的机会。这个小小形状，关系到很大的安全。
stories.answer_en: At high altitude, the pressure inside and outside an airplane is different, so the area around windows feels strong forces. Sharp corners concentrate stress, like paper tearing easily from a small cut. Rounded corners spread the force more evenly and reduce the chance of cracks starting there.
stories.fun: 工程设计里，很多圆角不是为了好看，而是为了让材料更安全。

stories.title_zh: 为什么电动车很安静，却有提示音？
stories.title_en: Why do quiet electric cars make warning sounds?
stories.answer_zh: 电动车低速行驶时没有明显发动机声音，行人，尤其是看不见或没注意路况的人，可能不容易发现车靠近。提示音能让人用耳朵知道附近有车。安静是优点，但在路上，适当被听见也是安全的一部分。
stories.answer_en: Electric cars can be very quiet at low speeds because they do not have a loud engine. Pedestrians, especially people who cannot see well or are distracted, may not notice them approaching. Warning sounds help people hear that a vehicle is nearby. Being quiet is nice, but being heard can be safer.
stories.fun: 未来城市也许会认真设计“好听但不吵”的车辆提示音。

categories.title: 城市与材料
categories.subtitle: 桥、路、楼房和日用品里的科学

stories.title_zh: 为什么桥常用拱形和三角形？
stories.title_en: Why do bridges use arches and triangles?
stories.answer_zh: 拱形能把重量分散到两侧支撑，三角形不容易变形。工程师用这些形状让桥更稳、更省材料。
stories.answer_en: Arches spread weight to supports, and triangles resist changing shape. Engineers use them to make bridges stable with less material.
stories.fun: 很多铁塔里也藏着三角形。

stories.title_zh: 为什么高楼会轻微摇晃？
stories.title_en: Why do tall buildings sway a little?
stories.answer_zh: 风和地震会推高楼。完全僵硬的楼反而容易受损，允许小幅摆动能吸收一部分能量。
stories.answer_en: Wind and earthquakes push tall buildings. Slight sway helps absorb energy; a perfectly rigid building could be damaged more easily.
stories.fun: 这种摇晃通常很小，人不一定感觉得到。

stories.title_zh: 为什么楼房要打地基？
stories.title_en: Why do buildings need foundations?
stories.answer_zh: 地基把建筑重量分散到更深、更稳定的土层或岩层上，防止房子下沉、倾斜或开裂。
stories.answer_en: Foundations spread a building’s weight into deeper, more stable soil or rock, preventing sinking, tilting and cracking.
stories.fun: 地基看不见，却决定上面的楼稳不稳。

stories.title_zh: 为什么桥会留伸缩缝？
stories.title_en: Why do bridges have expansion joints?
stories.answer_zh: 材料受热会膨胀，遇冷会收缩。伸缩缝给桥面留一点活动空间，避免热胀冷缩把桥挤坏。
stories.answer_en: Materials expand in heat and shrink in cold. Expansion joints give bridge decks room to move so temperature changes do not crack them.
stories.fun: 路面上有些缝不是坏了，而是故意留下的。

stories.title_zh: 为什么高楼要装阻尼器？
stories.title_en: Why do tall buildings use dampers?
stories.answer_zh: 高楼遇到大风或地震时会轻微摇晃。阻尼器常常是一块很重的东西，能在楼晃动时朝相反方向运动，帮楼把一部分能量消耗掉。它像给高楼装了一个会保持冷静的“平衡伙伴”，让摇晃变小，人在里面更舒服也更安全。
stories.answer_en: Tall buildings can sway in strong wind or earthquakes. A damper is often a very heavy mass that moves against the building’s motion, using up some of the energy. It acts like a calm balancing partner for the tower, reducing movement and making the building safer and more comfortable.
stories.fun: 台北101大楼里就有一个著名的大型阻尼球，游客还能看到。

stories.title_zh: 为什么建筑要留伸缩缝？
stories.title_en: Why do buildings need expansion joints?
stories.answer_zh: 材料会热胀冷缩。夏天变热时，混凝土、钢材会稍微变长；冬天变冷时又会缩短。如果完全不给它们活动空间，墙面或地面可能被挤裂。伸缩缝像给建筑留的小呼吸口，让它能安静地变大变小一点点。
stories.answer_en: Materials expand when warm and shrink when cold. Concrete and steel become slightly longer in summer and shorter in winter. Without room to move, walls or floors can crack. Expansion joints are small breathing spaces that let a building change size quietly.
stories.fun: 桥梁和铁路也需要考虑热胀冷缩，不然夏天可能被挤弯。

stories.title_zh: 为什么玻璃幕墙有时会造成强反光？
stories.title_en: Why can glass buildings create strong glare?
stories.answer_zh: 大面积玻璃会反射阳光。如果玻璃表面有弧度或角度刚好合适，阳光可能被集中反射到某个地方，让那里特别刺眼甚至变热。玻璃建筑漂亮，但设计时要考虑太阳位置、行人眼睛和周围环境，不然城市会多出一面巨大的镜子。
stories.answer_en: Large glass surfaces reflect sunlight. If the glass is curved or angled in a certain way, sunlight can be concentrated into one area, making it very bright or even hot. Glass buildings can be beautiful, but designers must consider the sun, pedestrians, and surroundings.
stories.fun: 有些城市会限制建筑反光，因为强眩光会影响司机和行人。

stories.title_zh: 为什么楼梯台阶高度差不多？
stories.title_en: Why are stair steps usually the same height?
stories.answer_zh: 人走楼梯时，大脑和腿会很快形成节奏。如果某一级突然高一点或低一点，脚就容易踩空或绊倒。统一的台阶高度能让身体放心地重复动作。楼梯好不好走，不只看宽不宽，也看它有没有尊重人的步伐习惯。
stories.answer_en: When people walk on stairs, the brain and legs quickly form a rhythm. If one step is suddenly higher or lower, the foot may miss or trip. Equal step height lets the body repeat the movement safely. Good stairs respect the rhythm of human walking.
stories.fun: 你觉得某段楼梯“怪怪的”，可能真的是台阶比例不舒服。

stories.title_zh: 为什么桥下面要有桥墩？
stories.title_en: Why do bridges have pillars underneath?
stories.answer_zh: 桥要承受自己的重量、车和人的重量，还要面对风和水流。桥墩把这些力传到地下或河床深处，让桥面不至于像一块太长的尺子那样弯下去。桥墩不是随便立在那里，它们是桥把重量交给大地的手臂。
stories.answer_en: A bridge must carry its own weight, vehicles, people, wind, and sometimes water forces. Pillars transfer those loads into the ground or riverbed so the bridge deck does not sag like an overly long ruler. Pillars are the bridge’s arms handing weight to the Earth.
stories.fun: 很长的桥会用多种结构合作，比如桥墩、拱、斜拉索或悬索。

stories.title_zh: 为什么一些古建筑木结构很抗震？
stories.title_en: Why can some old wooden buildings resist earthquakes?
stories.answer_zh: 很多传统木结构不是把所有地方都固定死，而是用榫卯和框架让木头之间有一点点活动空间。地震来时，结构能晃动、变形、消耗能量，而不是立刻断裂。它像会弯腰的人，比僵硬站着的人更不容易被推倒。
stories.answer_en: Many traditional wooden structures are not completely rigid. Joints and frames allow small movements between pieces of wood. During an earthquake, the structure can sway, deform, and absorb energy instead of snapping immediately. It is like a person who bends rather than standing stiffly.
stories.fun: 柔并不等于弱，工程里“会动一点”有时正是强大的地方。

stories.title_zh: 为什么斑马线是白条？
stories.title_en: Why are crosswalks painted as white stripes?
stories.answer_zh: 白色和深色路面对比强，司机和行人都容易看见。重复条纹也会让大脑快速识别“这里能过街”。
stories.answer_en: White stripes contrast strongly with dark roads, making them easy to see. The repeated pattern signals a crossing area.
stories.fun: 交通设计也用到了视觉心理学。

stories.title_zh: 为什么红灯停绿灯行？
stories.title_en: Why do red lights mean stop and green lights mean go?
stories.answer_zh: 红色在雾和远距离中比较醒目，也常被人当作警告色。交通系统把颜色规则固定下来，让所有人形成共同语言。
stories.answer_en: Red is highly noticeable and often used as a warning colour. Traffic systems standardise colours so everyone shares the same rule.
stories.fun: 规则本身也是一种社会发明。

stories.title_zh: 为什么柏油路夏天会发软？
stories.title_en: Why can asphalt soften in summer?
stories.answer_zh: 柏油是一种会随温度变软的材料。太阳暴晒后路面温度很高，柏油变软，就可能留下车辙。
stories.answer_en: Asphalt softens with heat. Strong sunlight can make road surfaces very hot, so asphalt may deform under vehicles.
stories.fun: 热天闻到柏油味，也和小分子挥发有关。

stories.title_zh: 为什么轮胎要有花纹？
stories.title_en: Why do tyres have tread patterns?
stories.answer_zh: 花纹能把轮胎和地面之间的水排开，让橡胶更好接触路面，增加摩擦力，减少打滑。
stories.answer_en: Tread channels move water away between tyre and road, improving contact and friction and reducing slipping.
stories.fun: 光头胎在雨天很危险。

stories.title_zh: 为什么路面要做出一点坡度？
stories.title_en: Why are roads slightly sloped?
stories.answer_zh: 如果路面完全平，雨水容易停在上面，形成积水，车轮经过会打滑，也会让路面更快损坏。道路通常会做出一点点坡度，让水流向两边的排水口。这个坡度小到走路不太注意，却能在下雨时帮城市把水送走。
stories.answer_en: If a road were perfectly flat, rainwater would sit on it, causing puddles, slippery driving, and faster damage. Roads are usually built with a slight slope so water flows toward drains on the sides. The slope is subtle, but in rain it helps the city move water away.
stories.fun: 路边的雨水口不是装饰，它是道路的排水嘴巴。

stories.title_zh: 为什么隧道里声音特别大？
stories.title_en: Why is sound louder in a tunnel?
stories.answer_zh: 隧道的墙面硬，空间又像长管子，声音碰到墙后会反射回来，不容易散开。车声、脚步声、说话声在里面来回弹，就会显得更响、更混。隧道像一个长长的回声盒，把声音留住了一会儿。
stories.answer_en: Tunnel walls are hard, and the space is shaped like a long tube. Sound reflects from the walls and does not spread away easily. Vehicle noise, footsteps, and voices bounce around, making everything louder and messier. A tunnel is like a long echo box.
stories.fun: 隧道里按喇叭会特别刺耳，所以很多地方会提醒司机不要鸣笛。

stories.title_zh: 为什么高速路要有缓冲护栏？
stories.title_en: Why do highways have crash barriers?
stories.answer_zh: 车如果失控，直接撞上硬墙会在很短时间内停下，冲击力很大。护栏可以变形、弯曲或引导车辆方向，让车停下来的时间稍微变长，吸收一部分能量。多出来的那一点点时间，对安全非常重要。
stories.answer_en: If a car hits a hard wall and stops almost instantly, the force is huge. Crash barriers can bend, deform, or guide the vehicle, increasing the stopping time and absorbing some energy. That small extra amount of time matters greatly for safety.
stories.fun: 安全设计常常不是让碰撞完全消失，而是让能量更温和地被处理。

stories.title_zh: 为什么交通标志常用醒目的颜色？
stories.title_en: Why do traffic signs use bright colours?
stories.answer_zh: 开车和走路时，人要在很短时间内看懂信息。红色、黄色、蓝色等高对比颜色容易被注意到，也容易形成固定含义：红色常提醒停止或危险，黄色提醒小心，蓝色常给指引。颜色让大脑在读字之前就先收到提示。
stories.answer_en: Drivers and pedestrians must understand information quickly. High-contrast colours such as red, yellow, and blue are easy to notice and can carry familiar meanings: red often means stop or danger, yellow means caution, and blue often gives guidance. Colour gives the brain a clue before reading words.
stories.fun: 好的标志设计，要让人在远处、雨天、夜里都尽量看得懂。

stories.title_zh: 为什么夜光路牌会反光？
stories.title_en: Why do road signs reflect headlights at night?
stories.answer_zh: 很多路牌表面有微小玻璃珠或棱镜结构，能把车灯照来的光反射回司机方向。这和普通镜子不太一样，它更像会把光“送回去”的表面。于是夜里即使没有路灯，车灯一照，标志也能亮起来。
stories.answer_en: Many road signs contain tiny glass beads or prism structures that reflect headlights back toward the driver. This is different from an ordinary mirror; it is designed to send light back where it came from. So at night, signs can shine when headlights hit them.
stories.fun: 这种材料叫逆反射材料，很多安全背心上也有。

stories.title_zh: 为什么减速带能让车慢下来？
stories.title_en: Why do speed bumps make cars slow down?
stories.answer_zh: 车快速经过减速带时，轮子和车身会突然上下运动，乘客会不舒服，车也可能受冲击。司机为了让车平稳，就会自然减速。减速带不是用文字劝你慢，而是用路面的形状让速度太快变得不舒服。
stories.answer_en: When a car goes over a speed bump quickly, the wheels and body move sharply up and down. Passengers feel uncomfortable, and the car may be jolted. Drivers slow down to keep the ride smooth. A speed bump uses road shape, not words, to encourage slower driving.
stories.fun: 学校和小区常有减速带，因为那里更需要保护行人。

stories.title_zh: 为什么马路边要有雨水井？
stories.title_en: Why do roads need storm drains?
stories.answer_zh: 雨水如果留在路面，会让车打滑、行人积水。雨水井把水导入地下管道，让路面尽快恢复安全。
stories.answer_en: Rainwater left on roads causes slipping and flooding. Storm drains carry water into pipes so roads become safer faster.
stories.fun: 落叶堵住雨水井，积水会更严重。

stories.title_zh: 自来水为什么要消毒？
stories.title_en: Why is tap water disinfected?
stories.answer_zh: 河水或水库水里可能有微生物。水厂会过滤、沉淀并消毒，减少病菌，让水更安全。
stories.answer_en: River or reservoir water may contain microbes. Water plants filter, settle and disinfect it to make it safer.
stories.fun: 干净的自来水是现代城市很重要的科学成果。

stories.title_zh: 为什么水塔能让水流出来？
stories.title_en: Why can water towers supply water?
stories.answer_zh: 水塔把水放在高处，重力会给水压。打开水龙头时，高处的水就能被压力推到家里。
stories.answer_en: A water tower stores water high above ground. Gravity creates pressure that pushes water through pipes when taps open.
stories.fun: 高处的水自带压力。

stories.title_zh: 为什么下水道有弯弯的存水处？
stories.title_en: Why do drains have a U-shaped bend?
stories.answer_zh: 弯管里会留一小段水，像一扇水做的门，挡住下水道里的臭气回到房间。
stories.answer_en: The U-bend holds a little water, forming a water seal that blocks sewer smells from coming back indoors.
stories.fun: 长期不用的地漏会干，臭味就可能上来。

stories.title_zh: 为什么水龙头里会有起泡器？
stories.title_en: Why do taps have aerators?
stories.answer_zh: 很多水龙头口有一个小网状装置，会把空气混进水流里。这样水看起来更饱满，冲洗时不那么容易飞溅，还能减少用水量。起泡器像给水流加了许多小空气垫，让同样的清洗感觉用更少的水完成。
stories.answer_en: Many taps have a small mesh device that mixes air into the water flow. The stream feels fuller, splashes less, and uses less water. An aerator adds tiny air cushions to the stream, giving a useful flow while saving water.
stories.fun: 如果水龙头水流变乱，可能是起泡器里堵了水垢或小沙粒。

stories.title_zh: 为什么污水要处理后才能排放？
stories.title_en: Why must wastewater be treated before release?
stories.answer_zh: 生活污水里可能有食物残渣、油脂、清洁剂、细菌和许多看不见的污染物。如果直接排进河海，会消耗水里的氧气，伤害鱼虾，也可能让人接触病菌。污水处理厂像城市的肠胃，把脏水分步骤清理，再还给自然。
stories.answer_en: Household wastewater may contain food scraps, grease, detergents, bacteria, and invisible pollutants. If released directly into rivers or the sea, it can use up oxygen, harm animals, and spread germs. A wastewater plant works like a city’s digestive system, cleaning water step by step.
stories.fun: 处理后的水有时还能用于浇绿地、冲洗道路或工业冷却。

stories.title_zh: 为什么城市下大雨容易内涝？
stories.title_en: Why do cities flood during heavy rain?
stories.answer_zh: 森林和泥土地面能吸收一部分雨水，但城市里有很多柏油路、水泥地和屋顶，水很难渗进去，只能快速流向低处和排水管。如果雨下得太猛，排水系统来不及运走，水就会积起来。城市越硬，雨水越没有地方慢慢停留。
stories.answer_en: Forests and soil can absorb some rainwater, but cities have asphalt roads, concrete, and roofs. Water cannot soak in easily, so it rushes toward low areas and drains. If rain falls faster than the drainage system can carry it away, flooding happens. Hard cities give rain fewer places to pause.
stories.fun: 海绵城市会用透水地面、绿地和蓄水空间帮雨水慢下来。

stories.title_zh: 为什么消防栓能喷出很强的水？
stories.title_en: Why can fire hydrants spray powerful water?
stories.answer_zh: 消防栓连接着城市供水管网，管道里的水保持一定压力。消防员打开消防栓后，水会从高压区域冲出来，形成强水流。它就像城市提前把水存在一张有压力的管道网里，等火灾时快速拿出来使用。
stories.answer_en: Fire hydrants are connected to a city’s water network, where water is kept under pressure. When firefighters open a hydrant, water rushes out from the pressurised pipes, creating a strong flow. The city keeps water ready in a pressured network for emergencies.
stories.fun: 消防栓附近不能乱停车，因为紧急时每一秒都重要。

stories.title_zh: 为什么水管冬天会冻裂？
stories.title_en: Why can water pipes burst in winter?
stories.answer_zh: 水有一个特别的习惯：结冰时体积会变大。水管里的水如果冻住，冰会占更多空间，挤压管壁。压力太大时，管子就可能裂开。等天气变暖冰融化，裂口处就会漏水，所以有些地方冬天要给水管保温。
stories.answer_en: Water has a special habit: it expands when it freezes. If water inside a pipe freezes, the ice takes up more space and presses on the pipe walls. If the pressure becomes too high, the pipe can crack. When ice melts later, water leaks through the crack.
stories.fun: 寒冷地区的室外水管常会包保温材料，像给管子穿棉衣。

stories.title_zh: 为什么饮水机热水口有童锁？
stories.title_en: Why do hot-water dispensers have child locks?
stories.answer_zh: 热水烫伤只需要很短时间，孩子可能因为好奇或误碰按到热水按钮。童锁让出热水需要多一个动作，比如按住再推，降低误触机会。这是设计在替人提前想：当危险很快发生时，不要只靠“我会小心”。
stories.answer_en: Hot water can burn skin very quickly, and children may press a button out of curiosity or by accident. A child lock adds an extra action, such as pressing and pushing, making accidental use less likely. Good design plans for mistakes instead of only saying “be careful.”
stories.fun: 很多安全设计的目标，不是让聪明人更聪明，而是让普通失误不变成大事故。

stories.title_zh: 为什么塑料又轻又能做很多形状？
stories.title_en: Why is plastic light and easy to shape?
stories.answer_zh: 许多塑料由长链分子组成，密度较低，加热后会变软，能被压成瓶子、盒子、玩具等形状。
stories.answer_en: Many plastics are made of long-chain molecules, are low in density and soften with heat, so they can be moulded into many shapes.
stories.fun: 方便不代表能乱丢，塑料垃圾很难消失。

stories.title_zh: 为什么玻璃透明？
stories.title_en: Why is glass transparent?
stories.answer_zh: 可见光穿过玻璃时，不容易被玻璃内部的电子吸收，所以大部分光能通过，我们就能看见后面的东西。
stories.answer_en: Visible light passes through glass because its electrons do not easily absorb that light, so most light gets through.
stories.fun: 毛玻璃不是不透明材料变了，而是表面把光散乱了。

stories.title_zh: 为什么纸能吸水？
stories.title_en: Why can paper absorb water?
stories.answer_zh: 纸由许多植物纤维压成，纤维之间有细小缝隙。水会被这些缝隙和纤维表面拉进去。
stories.answer_en: Paper is made of plant fibres with tiny gaps. Water is pulled into the gaps and onto fibre surfaces.
stories.fun: 纸巾比打印纸更蓬松，所以吸水更快。

stories.title_zh: 为什么橡皮筋能拉长又缩回？
stories.title_en: Why can a rubber band stretch and return?
stories.answer_zh: 橡胶分子像许多卷曲长线。拉伸时线被拉直，松手后它们又想卷回去，所以橡皮筋缩回。
stories.answer_en: Rubber molecules are like coiled long threads. Stretching straightens them; when released, they curl back.
stories.fun: 橡皮筋老化后分子受损，就容易断。

stories.title_zh: 为什么钢筋和混凝土常常一起用？
stories.title_en: Why are steel bars and concrete used together?
stories.answer_zh: 混凝土很擅长承受挤压，但不太擅长被拉伸；钢筋很擅长承受拉力。把钢筋埋进混凝土里，两者就能互相补短：混凝土负责抗压，钢筋负责抗拉。它们像一组搭档，一个稳，一个韧。
stories.answer_en: Concrete is strong under compression but weaker under tension. Steel bars are strong under tension. When steel is placed inside concrete, they help each other: concrete handles squeezing forces, and steel handles pulling forces. They work like partners, one steady and one tough.
stories.fun: 很多楼房和桥梁看起来是水泥，其实里面藏着钢筋骨架。

stories.title_zh: 为什么泡沫箱能保温？
stories.title_en: Why can foam boxes keep things warm or cold?
stories.answer_zh: 泡沫材料里有许多小小的空气格子。空气如果被困在小格子里，就不容易流动，热量也不容易快速传走。泡沫箱不是自己会制造冷或热，而是让里面和外面交换热量的速度变慢。
stories.answer_en: Foam contains many tiny pockets of trapped air. When air is held in small spaces, it cannot move around easily, so heat travels through it more slowly. A foam box does not create cold or heat; it slows heat exchange between inside and outside.
stories.fun: 羽绒服保暖也和困住空气有关。

stories.title_zh: 为什么陶瓷碗很硬却怕摔？
stories.title_en: Why is a ceramic bowl hard but easy to break?
stories.answer_zh: 陶瓷很硬，表面不容易被刮花，但它比较脆，不擅长弯曲。摔到地上时，撞击造成的小裂缝会迅速扩大，碗就碎了。硬和韧不是一回事：玻璃、陶瓷很硬，橡胶不硬，却更能变形不碎。
stories.answer_en: Ceramic is hard, so its surface is not easily scratched. But it is brittle and does not bend well. When dropped, tiny cracks from the impact can grow quickly, and the bowl breaks. Hardness and toughness are different: glass and ceramic are hard, while rubber is softer but bends without shattering.
stories.fun: 工程师选材料时，不会只问“硬不硬”，还要问“会不会脆”。

stories.title_zh: 为什么橡胶鞋底比较防滑？
stories.title_en: Why do rubber soles grip well?
stories.answer_zh: 橡胶比较软，踩到地面时能稍微变形，贴进地面的小凹凸里，接触面积变大，摩擦力也更强。鞋底花纹还能把水排开，减少一层滑滑的水膜。防滑不是单靠材料，也靠形状和地面的配合。
stories.answer_en: Rubber is relatively soft. When it touches the ground, it deforms a little and fits into tiny bumps, increasing contact and friction. Tread patterns also move water away, reducing a slippery water film. Grip comes from both material and shape.
stories.fun: 鞋底磨平后更容易滑，因为花纹排水和抓地能力都变差了。

stories.title_zh: 为什么铝易拉罐很轻却不容易漏？
stories.title_en: Why are aluminium cans light but not leaky?
stories.answer_zh: 铝很轻，而且可以被压成很薄的片。易拉罐的圆柱形和弯曲边缘能把压力分散，让薄薄的金属也很结实。铝表面还会形成一层很薄的氧化膜，帮助保护里面的金属。轻巧的罐子，其实用了不少形状智慧。
stories.answer_en: Aluminium is light and can be made into thin sheets. A can’s cylinder shape and curved edges spread forces, making thin metal surprisingly strong. Aluminium also forms a thin oxide layer that protects the metal underneath. A light can uses clever shape as well as material.
stories.fun: 空易拉罐从侧面一捏容易瘪，从上下方向却能承受不少重量。

stories.title_zh: 为什么雨衣能防水？
stories.title_en: Why can a raincoat keep water out?
stories.answer_zh: 雨衣表面通常有不容易被水浸湿的涂层，水滴落上去会聚成小珠滚走，而不是钻进布料缝隙。好的雨衣还要处理接缝和拉链，因为水最喜欢从小缝里找路。防水不是一整块布的事情，而是每个边角都要守住。
stories.answer_en: A raincoat usually has a coating that water does not wet easily. Raindrops bead up and roll away instead of soaking into the fabric. Good raincoats also protect seams and zippers because water loves finding small gaps. Waterproofing is about every edge, not just the fabric.
stories.fun: 有些透气雨衣能让水汽出去，却不让雨滴进来，这是材料孔洞大小在帮忙。

stories.title_zh: 为什么下水道井盖多是圆的？
stories.title_en: Why are manhole covers often round?
stories.answer_zh: 圆形井盖无论怎么转，都不会掉进同样圆的洞里；它还能滚动搬运，比较方便。
stories.answer_en: A round cover cannot fall through a round hole of the same size no matter how it turns, and it can be rolled.
stories.fun: 这是工程里很聪明的小设计。

stories.title_zh: 为什么拉链能合上又打开？
stories.title_en: How does a zipper open and close?
stories.answer_zh: 拉链两边有交错的小齿。拉头经过时，会把小齿按顺序扣在一起；反方向拉时又把它们分开。
stories.answer_en: A zipper has interlocking teeth. The slider presses them together in order, and moving back separates them.
stories.fun: 小小拉链其实是连续重复的机械动作。

stories.title_zh: 为什么雨伞骨架要分成很多根？
stories.title_en: Why do umbrellas have many ribs?
stories.answer_zh: 多根伞骨能把布撑成稳定曲面，也把风和雨的力分散开。只有一两根支撑，伞面很容易塌。
stories.answer_en: Many ribs hold the fabric in a stable curved shape and spread wind and rain forces. One or two supports would collapse easily.
stories.fun: 大风天伞翻过去，是风力超过结构能承受的结果。

stories.title_zh: 为什么书包有很多隔层？
stories.title_en: Why do backpacks have compartments?
stories.answer_zh: 隔层能把重量分散，防止物品乱晃，也让常用东西更容易找到。好的设计是在帮助身体和大脑省力。
stories.answer_en: Compartments distribute weight, stop items from shifting, and make things easier to find. Good design saves effort for body and brain.
stories.fun: 设计不只是好看，还要让人用得舒服。

stories.title_zh: 为什么门把手在远离合页的一边？
stories.title_en: Why is a door handle far from the hinges?
stories.answer_zh: 门绕着合页转动。你推的地方离合页越远，同样的力就越容易把门转开，这叫力矩。把手放在远离合页的一边，是为了让开门更省力。如果把手装在合页旁边，你会发现门突然变得很“倔”。
stories.answer_en: A door rotates around its hinges. The farther you push from the hinges, the easier it is to turn the door with the same force. This turning effect is called torque. Placing the handle far from the hinges makes opening the door easier.
stories.fun: 扳手越长越省力，也是同一个道理。

stories.title_zh: 为什么书页边角会卷起来？
stories.title_en: Why do book page corners curl?
stories.answer_zh: 纸是由细小纤维组成的，会吸收空气里的水分。书页一面吸湿多、一面吸湿少，或者手指经常摸某个角，纤维膨胀不均匀，边角就会弯起来。纸看起来安静，其实一直在和空气里的湿度悄悄互动。
stories.answer_en: Paper is made of tiny fibres that absorb moisture from the air. If one side absorbs more moisture than the other, or fingers often touch one corner, the fibres swell unevenly and the corner curls. Paper seems quiet, but it is always interacting with humidity.
stories.fun: 潮湿天气里，纸张和木门都可能变得不太听话。

stories.title_zh: 为什么铅笔能写字？
stories.title_en: Why can a pencil write?
stories.answer_zh: 铅笔芯主要是石墨和黏土，不是铅。石墨是一层层排列的材料，层与层之间容易滑开。你写字时，纸面的小纤维把石墨薄片刮下来，留在纸上形成黑色痕迹。铅笔写字，其实是在纸上铺下很薄的石墨。
stories.answer_en: Pencil “lead” is mostly graphite and clay, not lead. Graphite is made of layers that slide apart easily. When you write, tiny fibres in the paper scrape off thin flakes of graphite, leaving dark marks. A pencil writes by laying a very thin layer of graphite on paper.
stories.fun: 铅笔标号里的B越多通常越黑越软，H越多通常越硬越浅。

stories.title_zh: 为什么橡皮能擦掉铅笔字？
stories.title_en: Why can an eraser remove pencil marks?
stories.answer_zh: 铅笔字是石墨薄片附在纸纤维上。橡皮摩擦纸面时，会把石墨从纸上拉下来，并把它带进橡皮屑里。橡皮不是让字消失，而是把石墨搬走了。擦太用力会伤纸，因为纸纤维也会被一起扯起来。
stories.answer_en: Pencil marks are graphite flakes attached to paper fibres. When an eraser rubs the paper, it pulls graphite away and traps it in eraser crumbs. The eraser does not make the writing vanish; it carries the graphite away. Rubbing too hard can damage paper fibres.
stories.fun: 圆珠笔难擦，是因为墨水会渗进纸纤维里，不只是躺在表面。

stories.title_zh: 为什么魔术贴能反复粘？
stories.title_en: Why can Velcro stick again and again?
stories.answer_zh: 魔术贴一面有很多小钩，另一面有很多小环。压在一起时，小钩挂住小环；拉开时，许多小连接同时被扯开。因为这些钩和环没有用胶水，所以可以重复使用很多次。它像一片微型森林和一群小钩子互相抓住。
stories.answer_en: Velcro has many tiny hooks on one side and loops on the other. When pressed together, hooks catch loops. When pulled apart, many small connections release at once. Because it does not rely on glue, it can be reused many times.
stories.fun: 魔术贴的灵感来自会粘在衣服上的植物小刺球。

stories.title_zh: 为什么拉杆箱有轮子就省力？
stories.title_en: Why is a suitcase easier to move with wheels?
stories.answer_zh: 如果没有轮子，你要把箱子在地面上拖动，滑动摩擦很大。有轮子后，箱子主要是在滚动，滚动摩擦通常小得多。轮子把“硬拉一块重东西”变成“让它自己滚过去”，所以手臂轻松很多。
stories.answer_en: Without wheels, you drag a suitcase across the ground, creating large sliding friction. With wheels, the suitcase rolls, and rolling friction is usually much smaller. Wheels turn “dragging a heavy object” into “letting it roll,” so your arm works much less.
stories.fun: 轮子越顺、地面越平，拉杆箱越像自己愿意往前走。
